101.Title: Must-Read for Returnees
Content:
Q: How to catch up in level?
A: The main source of EXP is [Story Quests] and daily [Activity] rewards in the Season Hub. Other [Quests] also grant EXP. The daily [Activity] EXP is stored in the [Unclaimed EXP Pool], and future EXP gains will be doubled until you've caught up. The level cap for Season 1 is 60. Returning Adventurers can quickly catch up through daily activity and main story quests.

Q: How to recover lost resources?
A: During your absence, the system will store missed [Daily Activity Rewards], contents from the [Honor Coin Store], [Unstable Space], [Stimen Vaults], overflowed Elite/Boss Loot Keys, and overflowed Focus. After returning, you can go to [Season Hub - Activity] and access the Reclaim Hub at the bottom left to recover them. Recovering resources helps you quickly catch up in strength.

Q: How to improve strength after returning?
A: Use [Unclaimed EXP] to quickly catch up on Adventurer Level, and retrieve resources from the [Reclaim Hub]. Then, use [Alloy Shards] to exchange for gear, and enhance it using recovered Reforge Stones and refining materials. Use recovered skill books and Imprint materials to develop your skills and talents, boosting your strength.
Tips: For more detailed progression guidance, refer to the [Must-Read for Newbies] section.

Q: Can I participate in gameplay before catching up in strength?
A: Most gameplay modes that provide resources can be completed solo and do not require high strength. Examples include: Daily Missions, Unstable Space, Field Elite/Boss hunts, and Life Skills. In addition, as the season progresses, Chaotic Realm will offer AI Assistance mode for various difficulties to help Adventurers continue progressing in gear. Meanwhile, once strength improves, Stimen Vaults can also be challenged at higher floors (solo challenge allowed). Overall, after returning, Adventurers can participate in most content through solo activity and gradually catch up with the main group to resume regular play.
Tips: For information on Adventurer Level, progression systems, earning money, currencies, and key stores, please check the [Must-Read for Newbies] section.

1001.Title: Class Selection
Content: When the game begins, you've already chosen a Class you like. Once your Adventurer Level reaches Lv.9, you can unlock New Class through Guide Quests. Complete quests to unlock Classes and explore different combat styles, making your adventure even more dynamic.

1002.Title: Ability Score
Content: Ability Score reflects your <style="GuideBookLab1">overall progression</style> as an<style="GuideBookLab1">adventurer</style>. Increasing your <style="GuideBookLab1">Adventurer Level</style>, upgrading <style="GuideBookLab1">Talents,</style> <style="GuideBookLab1">Skills</style> (including Class Skills and Battle Imagines), <style="GuideBookLab1">Gear,</style> <style="GuideBookLab1">Modules,</style> and <style="GuideBookLab1">Deep-Slumber Psychoscope</style>—all contribute to raising your Ability Score.

1003.Title: Class Talents
Content:
Each class comes with its own unique set of Talents. Choose your preferred combat specialization and allocate your Talent Points wisely to improve your battle performance!
[Resources]
Talent Points: Adventurer Level-up, Trial Path, Talent Training Quests from Airona.
Sigil: Obtain through Colorful Store - Honor Coin Store, Trial Path rewards, Wild Boss/Elite Loot.
Tip: You can use "Auto-Activate" feature to quickly activate the recommended Talent Nodes. You can also view Spec details in Guide.

1004.Title: Skill Training
Content:
Equip your favorite Class Skills & upgrade and advance them to significantly boost your combat potential!
[Resources]
Skill book: Colorful Store - Honor Coin Store, Basic Star Chest, Trading Center, Mystery Store, C Exchange.
Tip: After obtaining skill books of other Classes, give them to Milia, the Class guide at the Pioneer Bureau, and exchange for Class skill books you need.

1005.Title: Battle Imagine
Content:
Each Battle Imagine includes one Active Skill and at least one Passive Effect. These skills can be advanced.
Match Battle Imagines to your role (Attack, Support, or Guard) for maximum efficiency in battle! [Battle Will] is crucial for Battle Imagine crafting.
[Resources]
Battle Will: Trading Center, Battle Will Exchange, Dismantle, Stimen Vaults Reward, Will Wish, Monster Hunt - Boss Chest.
Tip: Mason's Imagine Research Institute offers Imagine crafting, Imagine decomposition, and Battle Will exchange services.

1006.Title: Gear Upgrade
Content:
Equip higher-quality and higher-level gear to gain stronger attributes and combat performance. You can use Reforge to improve its perfection level and optimize it for various combat scenarios.
[Resources]
Gear: Unstable Space rewards, Chaotic Realm rewards, Monster Hunt rewards, Gear Exchange at the gear forging Artisan [Harvey].
Tip: Navigate [Gear Store] or [Gear Workshop] on the Map, then interact with the corresponding NPC to proceed.

1007.Title: Reforge
Content:
Reforge gear to enhance its attributes and effects. The first reforge unlocks a reforging attribute under Advanced Attributes, while subsequent reforge will steadily improve the gear's perfection level. For gear below the max perfection level, Reforge enhances basic and advanced attributes. However, once perfection is capped, Reforge will only refresh the reforging attribute.
Notably, when reforged gear is dismantled, reforge materials will be refunded, making it risk-free to reforge pre-capped gear.
Tip: Consume Reforge Stone to improve gear perfection (Material Sources: Unstable Space/Chaotic Realm/Honor Coin)

1008.Title: Refine
Content:
Refine to boost gear's performance. While refinement may succeed or fail, failure won't decrease the level.
Using Blessing Items increases your success rate.
When replacing gear of the same part, refinement will be automatically and freely transferred.
Tip: Refinement enhances the performance of gear part. (Material Sources: Honor Coin, Guild Grocery Store, Life Skill)

1013.Title: Embed
Content:
Embedding gems/sigils into gear can grant the gear the attributes that they carry, and choosing intermediate or advanced embedding methods can directly obtain higher-level gem/sigil effects.
Tip: Embedding gems/sigils can improve gear attributes. (Material Source: Life Skill)

1009.Title: Module
Content:
Equip Modules to activate Power Core Effects and grow even stronger!
Note: Only equipped modules take effect, and different modules may contain the same type of Power Core. The total number of successful links determines whether the Power Core's level effects are activated!

1010.Title: Adventure Level
Content:
Earn EXP to increase your Adventurer Level. This not only boosts your basic attributes, but also unlocks level rewards and new features!
Complete Quests, Seasonal Activity Goals, and defeat monsters to gain EXP and increase Adventurer Level.

1011.Title: Title Promotion
Content: Climb the ranks to unlock <style="GuideBookLab1">season-exclusive</style> <style="GuideBookLab1">fashion items</style> and <style="GuideBookLab1">emblems</style>!

1012.Title: Emblem Upgrade
Content: Upgrade power node, unlock and enhance Emblem Core, and collect tailored Emblem Core Entries to dramatically increase your combat power this season.

1014.Title: Deep-Slumber Psychoscope
Content: The Psychoscope system is composed of the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope and the Slumberdream Psychoscope. Each Psychoscope contains multiple Mind Projection areas. Adventurers can select and activate <style="accent-gn">1</style> area under each Psychoscope.

Each Mind Projection area contains multiple <style="accent-gn">Anchors of the Mind</style>, <style="accent-gn">Phantom Cursors</style>, and <style="accent-gn">Psychogram Imprint</style> nodes (areas under the Slumberdream Psychoscope do not have Psychogram Imprint nodes). By cultivating these nodes, adventurers can enhance their strength. (Note: You must <style="accent-gn">activate</style> the projection for it to <style="accent-gn">take effect</style>.)
Anchors of the Mind have fixed effects and can be activated by consuming items.
Each Phantom Cursor is essentially a slot for embedding Phantom Factors. When slotted, the Factor's effects apply to this projection.

Each Mind Projection area carries one Psychogram Imprint effect that allows insertion of an activated Battle Imagine. (Note: Inserted Battle Imagines remain usable and are not affected by this system.)
Battle Imagines provide Bond Points to the Psychogram Imprint. When the total Bond Points across all Psychogram Imprints in that projection reach 2/5/12/20/25/35, the effects of the corresponding Psychogram Imprint level is activated.

1015.Title: Regional Reputation Level
Content:
For adventurers, gaining Reputation EXP and raising its level is crucial! It unlocks valuable rewards and allows you to exchange abundant resources in the shop, paving the way for your accelerated growth.
Different regions maintain independent Reputation systems, and each region's Reputation EXP must be earned separately.
Complete regional Quests or participate in Illuminate the Journey activities to easily earn Reputation EXP and steadily increase your Reputation level.

1016.Title: Modification Guide
Content:
Modification is divided into Reforge Effect Modification and Rare Effect Modification.
Reforge Effect Modification: When gear reaches maximum Perfection, it can no longer be reforged. If the current reforge effects are unsatisfactory, one effect can be manually selected from the gear's reforge effect pool.
Rare Effect Modification: If the current rare effect is unsatisfactory, one effect can be manually selected from the gear's rare effect pool. Some gear does not initially have a rare effect, but one can still be selected from the gear's rare effect pool through Modification.

20010.Title: Gathering Life Skills
Content: Gathering Life Skills include <style="GuideBookLab1">Botany, Mineralogy, and Gemology</style>. When a life skill is first unlocked, you receive 3 Mastery points, which are shared across all life skills. You <style="GuideBookLab1">gain 1 additional point per 300 Focus consumed</style>.

Unlocking a gathering target requires a <style="GuideBookLab1">Mastery</style> point. You can reset this skill's Mastery by using the Reset button at the bottom left. <style="GuideBookLab1">All Mastery points will be refunded</style>, but it costs a Reset Ticket.

Gathering targets are divided into Focused and Regular. Regular doesn't consume Focus, but its products are untradeable-mainly used for crafting gear and furniture. Select an unlocked gathering target and use Track to view its location.

You can view the rewards of Focused and Regular. Some items only offer Regular rewards. The icons on the world map indicate where you can find the gathering targets. Head to those spots to gather materials.

When you are within range of a gathering item, a gathering icon indicating its type will appear above it. Approach it to trigger an interaction button and begin gathering.
There are two gathering modes: Focused and Regular. Focused consumes Focus, while Regular does not.
All gathering activities grant EXP for the corresponding gathering classes, but the final rewards vary based on the method used.

20011.Title: Crafting Life Skills
Content: Crafting Life Skills include <style="GuideBookLab1">Smelting, Culinary, Alchemy, Gemcrafting, Artisanry</style>, and <style="GuideBookLab1">Weaving</style>. When a life skill is first unlocked, you receive 3 Mastery points, which are shared across all life skills. You <style="GuideBookLab1">gain 1 additional point per 300 Focus consumed</style>.

Unlocking one or more crafting recipes requires a <style="GuideBookLab1">Mastery</style> point. You can reset this skill's Mastery by using the Reset button at the bottom left. <style="GuideBookLab1">All Mastery points will be refunded</style>, but it costs a Reset Ticket.
Tap the filter button at the bottom left to open the filter menu on the right. You can select/deselect recipe types: Materials, Consumables, Gear, and Furniture. Filter recipes to suit your needs.

Recipes are divided into the Focused and Regulars. Regulars do not consume Focus but produce untradeable results. Furniture must be crafted at the Crafting Station in the Homestead. Here you can only view recipes and unlock requirements. Select an unlocked recipe and go to start tracking the Crafting Station location for that life skill.
Proceed to the marked location on the world map.

Once you're near the target, an interaction button will appear. Open the crafting interface and view the unlocked recipes and required materials. If you have enough materials, use the Craft button below to create the item and gain Skill EXP.
Some crafting recipes consume Focus during production, while others require no Focus.

6070.Title: Fishing
Content:
[Fishing Bobber]
After casting your line, the bobber will float on the water's surface. When a fish is about to bite, the bobber will briefly sink.
Once a fish bites, the bobber will stay submerged. At this moment, Reel in to hook the fish and enter the fish-wrestling phase.

[Line Tension]
Reel in, and to line tension gradually increases.
The water is divided into three zones: left, center, and right. You need to align your fishing pole with the fish's position to reduce the tension, otherwise, the tension increases.
If the line remains fully tense for too long, it will snap, causing the fish to escape.

[Fishing Progress]
When your pole direction matches the fish's position, tapping or holding reel-in will increase your fishing progress and the fish icon moves leftward. When the icon reaches the far left, the fish is successfully caught.

6071.Title: Homestead
Content:
Adventurers can craft furniture, decorate, and plant flowers in the Homestead, and even share homestead with other adventurers!
Complete the Homestead tutorial quest and obtain the Island Ownership to move into the Homestead.

Use the Crafting Station to make furniture. Some furniture requires unlocking the crafting recipe first.
The Crafting Station starts with 4 crafting slots. The more adventurers share homestead, the more slots will become available!

Adventurers can decorate both the interior and exterior of the Homestead. Select the furniture you want to place and arrange it freely. Furniture supports full 360° rotation and height adjustment, and allows overlapping placement. It's recommended to use <style="GuideBookLab1">Snap, Grid, and Modular</style> functions to improve efficiency.

You can sow flower seeds in the Fields. Flower growth has three stages: Growth, Pollination, and Harvest. In the Growth stage, watering and fertilizing will speed it up. In the Pollination stage, you can apply pollen for artificial breeding. If no pollination occurs during the Pollination stage, the flower will passively pollinate from flowers in adjacent plots. If no neighboring flowers exist, it will inherit the seed's original traits. In the Harvest stage, you can harvest the mature flowers.
The color and shape of the flowers will be influenced by both seeds and pollen. The rarer the flower, the higher its value! Patience and experimentation are key to breeding rare varieties.

The Traveling Merchant periodically requests materials and furniture. Selling to them earns Homestead Coins, which can be used to purchase seeds, materials, and furniture in the Homestead Store.
Each adventurer can submit up to 3 commissions per day. Unsubmitted commissions roll over to the next day, up to 9.

Homestead cleanliness decreases over time. As a result, debris will appear around the Homestead. Cleaning up this debris will <style="GuideBookLab1">restore cleanliness</style>. You can also ask your butler to clean for you!

2000.Title: Skill Type
Content:
Each class features a variety of skill types:

1. Basic Attack - Typically used to generate class combat resources.
2. Special Attack - Core skills that consume combat resources.
3. Ultimates - The most powerful abilities of a class.
4. Class Skills - Choose any 3 from your skill library to define your unique playstyle.

Battle Imagine Skills

Unlock special monster-inspired skills via Battle Imagines. You can equip up to 2 and use them in battle once activated.
Match them to your role (Attack, Support, or Defense) for optimal results in combat.

2001.Title: Skill Activation Types
Content:
Icons next to skill buttons indicate how the skill is triggered.

The double arrow pointing right indicates a Combo Skill.
The number above the icon indicates a Charge Skill.

Combo Skill

These skills can be used multiple times in sequence. After the first activation, a countdown appears. Trigger again before the timer ends to trigger the next stage.

Note: If you don't trigger in time or the combo ends naturally, the chain ends.

Charge Skill

The number indicates how many charges are available for consecutive use.

Note: Multiple charges will not recover. Every time a charge CD finishes, only one charge is restored.

2002.Title: Combat Resources
Content:
During combat, you can spend resources to boost combat power or unleash powerful skills.

Note: Resource generation and consumption methods vary depending on the weapon.

2003.Title: Tags
Content: Tags indicate a skill's effect category. Only upgrades that match a skill's tag will enhance it.

2004.Title: Perfect Dodge
Content: Dodging an enemy attack at the right moment triggers a Perfect Dodge, allowing you to <style="GuideBookLab1">avoid incoming DMG</style> and empower your next basic attack.
Some Elite and Boss enemies use <style="GuideBookLab1">Lethal Skill</style>, signaled by a red glow.

Counterattack

Trigger a Perfect Dodge the moment a Lethal Skill lands to counter it. This interrupts the attack and puts the enemy into a weakened state.

Note: A successful counterattack lets you completely avoid the incoming DMG.

2005.Title: Super Armor Gauge
Content:
Elite and boss enemies have a unique mechanic-successful hits reduce their Super Armor effectiveness.
When depleted, it triggers a Break that instantly interrupts their action and weakens them for a short time.
Crowd Control skills are especially effective at reducing this gauge.

2006.Title: Dodgeable Warnings
Content: A <style="GuideBookLab1">dodgeable</style> attack warning is indicated by a flowing red light. Make sure to move out of the danger zone before it reaches the outer edge.

2007.Title: Undodgeable Warning
Content: A <style="GuideBookLab1">Dodgeable</style> attack warning is indicated by a flowing red light. Make sure to move out of the danger zone before it reaches the outer edge.

2008.Title: Split Damage
Content: Sometimes, the enemy will perform a powerful attack at a single friendly target, however, its DMG <style="GuideBookLab1">splits</style> among all allies within the area.

2009.Title: Mechanic Timer
Content: When a boss begins channeling a powerful sustained skill, pay attention to the countdown timer and react accordingly.

2010.Title: Focus Marker
Content: Targets marked with a Focus Marker must be killed quickly. Failing to do so will often result in severe consequences.

2011.Title: Target Marker
Content: When a Target Marker appears above your head, prepare to respond to the incoming enemy skill.

2012.Title: Skill CD Timer
Content: For boss enemies, watch their skill CD timers to plan your moves in advance.

2201.Title: Stormblade - Iaido Slash Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Iaido Slash as the Special Attack. Core Build: Stacks a large amount of Thunder Sigils to deliver high burst DMG on a single skill cast.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Overdrive: Enhances Thunder Sigil acquisition
Flash Strike: Enhances core DMG dealt ability
Raijin Dash: Boosts area damage dealt over time
Volt Surge: Improves Thunder Sigil acquisition efficiency
Optional
True Sight: Enhances survivability
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Agility: Increases ATK and Attack SPD for Stormblade.
Advanced Attributes
Crit: Increases DMG dealt by Crit hits, starting at 150%.
Mastery: Skills that consume Thunder Sigil gain extra DMG dealt per sigil.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Super Critical, Stunt! Spiral Severance to temporarily boost Crit rate and Thunder damage.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2301.Title: Stormblade - Moonstrike Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Moonstrike as the Special Attack. Core Build: Consumes 3 Thunder Sigils to unleash a Special Attack, summoning Moonblades. Follows up with rapid attacks to extend the Moonblades' duration and trigger various additional effects.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Overdrive: Thunder Sigil acquisition
Thundercut: Enhances core DMG dealt ability
Scythe Wheel: Boosts area damage dealt over time
Stormflash: Increases benefits from Thunder Sigil consumption
Optional
True Sight: Enhances survivability
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Agility: Increases ATK and Attack SPD for Stormblade.
Advanced Attributes
Haste: Increases attack and casting speed.
Luck: Grants a chance to trigger a Lucky Strike when dealing DMG or healing.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Heavy Slash, Stunt! Spiral Severance to temporarily boost Expertise Skill and Thunder damage.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2202.Title: Frost Mage - Icicle Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Frost Lance as the Special Attack. Core Build: Improves Frost Crystal acquisition. Frost Crystal allows the Special Attack to bypass casting time.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Meteor Storm​: Boosts core DMG dealt ability and grants Frost Crystal
Permafrost: Grants Ice Energy and boosts Haste
Crystal Veil: Grants Frost Crystal, Fire-and-Forget
Blizzard: Enhances area damage dealt over time, Fire-and-Forget
Optional
Frost Shelter: Enhances survivability
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Intellect: Increases the Frost Mage's MATK and Resistance.
Advanced Attributes
Crit: Increases DMG dealt by Crit hits, starting at 150%.
Luck: Chance to trigger a Lucky Strike when dealing DMG or healing.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Super Critical, Stunt! Water of Silence to temporarily boost Crit rate and Ice damage.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2302.Title: Frost Mage - Frostbeam Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Frostbeam as the Special Attack. Core Build: Stacks Frost Crystal to boost Special Attack DMG, and consumes Frost Crystal to restore Ice Energy.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Maelstrom: Grants Ice Energy, Fire-and-Forget
Permafrost: Grants Ice Energy and boosts Haste
Tidepool: Releases and restores Ice Energy when using the Special Attack
Frozen Gale: Focuses on restoring Ice Energy, area damage
Optional
Frost Shelter: Enhances survivability
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Intellect: Increases the Frost Mage's MATK and Resistance.
Advanced Attributes
Haste: Increases casting speed, and shortens channel time.
Mastery: Boosts Ice Energy acquisition efficiency.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Boarrier Rush, Stunt! Water of Silence to temporarily boosts Special Attack and Ice damage.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2204.Title: Wind Knight - Vanguard Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Gale Thrust as the Special Attack. Core Build: Boosts Courage accumulation, allowing more frequent use of Gale Thrust. By maintaining high stacks of Sharp, you can trigger the core mechanic that consumes Courage to reset skill CDs, further boosting the frequency of Spiral Thrust.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Speeds up Courage accumulation and boosts strength: ​Galeform​
Enhances core DMG output: Spiral Thrust
Enhances wide-area DMG and helps gain Courage: Breach Pursuit
Strong DoT, fire-and-forget: Valor Cyclone
Optional
Enhances survivability: ​Aegis Gale​
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Strength: Increases Wind Knight's ATK and Armor.
Advanced Attribute
Haste: Increases Attack SPD and Casting SPD.
Mastery: Grants Wind Bonus.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
For short-term burst benefits from rapid hits and Wind DMG: Stunt! Predator Slash, Stunt! Whirlwind Strike.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2304.Title: Wind Knight - Skyward Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Skyfall as the Special Attack. Core Build: This build focuses on increasing the efficiency of gaining Sharp when casting Skyfall, allowing for more frequent use of Instant Edge. Courage is refunded after casting Instant Edge, so the build emphasizes a smooth rhythm between these two core skills.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Galeform: Speeds up Courage accumulation and boosts strength.
Instant Edge: Enhances core DMG output.
Falcon Toss: Increases area damage.
Sharp Impact: Boosts DMG output.
Optional
Aegis Gale: Improves survivability.
Breach Pursuit: Enhances wide-area damage and helps gain Courage.
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Strength: Increases Wind Knight's ATK and Armor.
Advanced Attributes
Crit: Increases bonus DMG on Crits (starts at 150%).
Luck: Grants a chance to trigger a Lucky Strike when dealing DMG or healing.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Super Critical or Stunt! Whirlwind Strike to temporarily boost Crit and Wind DMG.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2205.Title: Verdant Oracle - Smite Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Wild Bloom as the Special Attack. Core Idea: This build deals DMG while also providing healing effects.
| Recommended Class Skill Setup
Required
Feral Seed: Generates Energy and deals DMG.
Regen Pulse: Enhances healing and dispels debuffs.
Infusion: Boosts DMG output.
Nature Ward: Grants shields to all friendly units.
Optional Skills
Nourish: Provides long-lasting healing and allows for Fire-and-Forget use.
Grove Wish: Greatly enhances healing with continuous channeling.
Growth Arcane: Reduces your skill CDs.
| Focused Attributes for This Build:
Basic Attributes
Intellect: Increases Verdant Oracle's MATK and Resistance.
Advanced Attributes
Mastery: Increases Forest DMG.
Luck: Grants a chance to trigger a Lucky Strike when dealing DMG or healing.
| Recommended Battle Imagine Setup
Equip Stunt! Falling Star or Stunt! Boarrier Rush to temporarily boost Intellect and Special Attack DMG.
For other needs, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2209.Title: Heavy Guardian - Earthfort Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Shield Bash as the Special Attack. Core Build: This build absorbs DMG through shields while gaining bonus DMG when a Shield is active. Effective in all situations requiring DMG tolerance.
| Optimal Class Skill Build

Rage Burst: Grants shields, Attack DMG reduction, and deals area damage.

2309.Title: Heavy Guardian - Block Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Countercrush as the Special Attack. Core Build: This build enhances Block to steadily reduce Attack DMG taken. It performs especially well in situations where Attack DMG is dominant.
| Optimal Class Skill Build

2211.Title: Marksman - Wildpack Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Storm Arrow as the Special Attack. Core Build: This build enhances synergy with the Wild Wolf, aiming to maintain the Photon Reforge state as much as possible so that Double Arrow consistently fire as Storm Arrow. This increases strike frequency, accelerates the Wild Wolf's attacks, and allows the summoning of additional Wild Wolves and Phantom Direwolves.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Torrent Volley: Boosts DMG and restores Energy during Photon Reforge.
Arrow Rain: Generates Photon Energy.
Wildcall: Boosts Companions' DMG.
Focus: Increase Haste and Special Attack frequency.
Optional
Radiance Barrage: Boosts area damage and increases DMG dealt during Photon Reforge.
Powerdraw: Enhances Resilience breaking efficiency and speeds up charging during Photon Reforge.
Deter Shot: Boosts survivability.
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Agility: Increases Marksman's ATK and Attack SPD.
Advanced Attributes
Haste: Boosts Attack SPD and Casting SPD.
Mastery: Increases DMG dealt by Companions.
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Blink Ambush or Stunt! Lightning Orb to temporarily boost Agility and Light DMG.
To learn more, please go to Battle Imagine interface and use Filter to check the recommendation.

2311.Title: Marksman - Falconry Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Double Arrow as the Special Attack. Core Build: Enhances the ability to fight alongside the Falcon. Crit hits trigger coordinated attack from Falcon. Focuses on increasing Crit frequency.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required

Boosts area damage and increases DMG dealt during Photon Reforge: Radiance Barrage
Boosts area damage and charges more quickly while in Photon Reforge: Powerdraw
Increases Crit effects: Blast Shot
Optional
Enhances DMG, recovers energy in Photon Reforge state: Torrent Volley
Grants Photon: Arrow Rain
Enhances survivability: Deter Shot
| Main Attributes for Spec training
Basic Attribute
Agility: Increases Marksman's ATK and Attack SPD.
Advanced Attributes
Haste: Increases Attack SPD and Cast SPD.
Crit: Boosts DMG on Crit Hits (base bonus: 150%).
| Optimal Battle Imagine Build
Equip Stunt! Boarrier Rush, Stunt! Lightning Orb to temporarily boost Special Attack DMG and Light DMG.

2212.Title: Shield Knight - Recovery Spec
Content:
A Spec that uses Valor Bash as the Special Attack. Core Build: Increases DEF through Valor Bash, and maintains coverage of Radiant Shield to absorb half of the DMG taken. Casts Judgment to restore lost HP.
| Optimal Class Skill Build
Required
Shield Toss: Increase DMG Reduction
Judgment: Restores HP
Aegis Ward: Enhances Defensive Capability
Reckoning: Efficiently grants Holy Energy, area damage

Advanced Attributes

Content:
A Spec that uses Vanguard Strike as the Special Attack. Core Build: Gains Lightforged Barrier through Vanguard Strike. Increases your max HP and provides resources for your Special Attack. Maintains coverage of Radiant Shield to absorb half of the DMG taken. Casts Judgment to restore lost HP.

A spec utilizing Blazing Ascension as the Special Attack. Core build: By alternating between Special Attacks and different Class Skills, the Formless level is increased to accelerate skill rotation, providing a high DMG bonus while aiming to trigger more Formless Flame Slashes.
