5101.Title: Mahjong Guide
Content: Mahjong Tiles

Mahjong tiles are divided into 5 categories: Characters (Manzu), Dots (Pinzu), Bamboo (Souzu), Wind Tiles, and Dragon Tiles.
Sequence: Three consecutive numbered tiles of the same suit.
Triplet: Three identical tiles.
Pair: Two identical tiles.
Chi, Pon, Kan, and Melds

Chi: If a tile discarded by the player to your left completes a Sequence with two tiles in your hand, you can call "Chi" to claim it and form a Sequence.
Pon: If any player discards a tile identical to a Pair in your hand, you can call "Pon" to claim it and form a Triplet.
Kan: If another player discards a tile that matches a Triplet in your hand, you can call "Kan" to form an Open Kan. If you have four identical tiles in your hand, you can also declare a Closed Kan. A Kan is treated as a Triplet. Calling "Kan" requires drawing a replacement tile and discarding one.
Melds: After calling Chi, Pon, or Kan, the corresponding tiles must be revealed and placed aside. These displayed tiles are called "Melds". A Closed Kan is not considered a Meld. Some Yaku require a concealed hand (no Melds) or have reduced value if the hand has Melds.
Winning Hands
The ultimate goal of each hand is to win (Ron/Tsumo). Typically, you need to assemble 4 sets of Sequences or Triplets + 1 Pair, and satisfy at least one Yaku (Hand) condition to win.
Yaku

The Mahjong game uses Riichi Mahjong rules. In Riichi Mahjong, you must have a "Yaku" to win. There are many Yakus, and you can view Yaku information during the game. The three simplest Yakus are: Tanyao (All Simples), Riichi, and Yakuhai (Honor Tiles).
Yaku: Tanyao (All Simples)

All tiles numbered 1 and 9, Wind Tiles, and Dragon Tiles are collectively called Terminals and Honors. If your hand (including Melds) contains no Terminals or Honors, and you form 4 sets of Sequences or Triplets + 1 Pair, you can win.
Yaku: Riichi

If your hand is concealed (no Chi, Pon, or Kan) and you need just one more tile to complete 4 sets of Sequences or Triplets + 1 Pair, you can declare Riichi. After declaring Riichi, you must play automatically until you win or the hand ends.
Yaku: Honor Tiles (Yakuhai)

The Mahjong table has four winds: East, South, West, and North. Your seat corresponds to your Seat Wind. The current round is fixed as the East Round, so East is the Prevalent Wind. White, Green, and Red are always Dragon Tiles. Seat Wind Tiles, Prevalent Wind Tiles, and Dragon Tiles are all "Honor Tiles". If you have a Triplet of any Honor Tiles, plus 3 other Sequences or Triplets + 1 Pair, you can win. Honor Tiles Yaku is valid even with Melds (Chi/Pon/Kan).

6050.Title: Fishing
Content:
[Fishing Bobber]
After casting your line, the bobber will float on the water's surface. When a fish is about to bite, the bobber will briefly sink.
Once a fish bites, the bobber will stay submerged. At this moment, tap to reel in and enter the fish-wrestling phase.

[Line Tension]
Tap or hold the reel-in button to gradually increase line tension.
The water is divided into three zones: left, center, and right. You need to align your fishing pole with the fish's position to reduce the tension, otherwise, the tension increases.
If the line remains fully tense for too long, it will snap, causing the fish to escape.

[Fishing Progress]
When your pole direction matches the fish's position, tap or hold reel-in to increase your fishing progress and make the fish icon move leftward. When the icon reaches the far left, the fish is successfully caught.
If the fishing progress does not increase after a certain period of time, it will start to decrease. When the progress drops to 0, the fish will break free after a period of time, resulting in a failed fishing attempt.

6001.Title: Treasure Chest
Content:
Gifts granted to adventurers on their journey. Open them to receive generous rewards.
Explore the world actively to uncover their whereabouts.

300291.Title: Renewal Light Wish Gameplay Instructions
Content: Renewal Light Limited Wish

To welcome the arrival of the new year, the Meowlux Merchant Guild presents the Renewal Festival limited-time Wish event. This event requires the use of the special Renewal Light Wish Coin which is only valid during the Renewal Festival. These Wish Coins can be obtained in the Renewal Store using Renewal Seals, or you can spend Rose Orbs x80 to purchase it directly.
Prizes & Pity

Prizes are divided into four tiers by grade. The rarer the prize, the lower the chance of getting it.
A pity system is in place. If you haven't received an [SSR] after a certain number of wishes, you'll be guaranteed to obtain an [SSR] once the wish count reaches the pity threshold.
Pity Carryover
The pity count for this Wish is calculated independentlyand will not carry over to other Wish events, nor affect their pity counts.
Prize Binding Rules

All rewards obtained from this Wish are bound items and cannot be listed or sold on the Trading Center.
Limited-time Appearance items under [SSR] cannot be obtained through other means after this event ends.
Limited-time appearance items under [SR] and [R] can also be obtained through exchange in the Renewal Store, in addition to drawing them.
Renewal Light Vault Card

The prizes obtained from <color=#FF8000>[SSR]</color> and <color=#eed335>[SR]</color> can be recycled at Kalista in the Meowlux Member Store on the second floor of the shopping center near the Pioneer Bureau for <color=#9cb9ef>Renewal Light Vault Cards</color>. When obtaining a duplicate Appearance through <color=#ab85f6>[R] Wishes</color>, it will automatically be converted into a certain number of Renewal Light Vault Cards, which can be used to exchange for rare items at the Renewal Store. After the event ends at 05:00 on March 9, 2026 (UTC-2), <color=#ffe2af>Kalista</color> will continue to maintain the Renewal Store access for exchanging for Renewal Light Vault Cards and other Renewal Light Wish rewards.
Prizes & Pity

Prizes are divided into four tiers by grade. The rarer the prize, the lower the chance of getting it.
A pity system is in place. If you haven't received an [SSR] after a certain number of wishes, you'll be guaranteed to obtain an [SSR] once the wish count reaches the pity threshold.
Pity Carryover
The pity count for this Wish is calculated independentlyand will not carry over to other Wish events, nor affect their pity counts.
Prize Binding Rules

6002.Title: Wind Barrier
Content:
Excerpt 1 from "A Study on Unique Species of Asteria Plains"
[Wind Barrier]: One of the unique phenomena of Asteria. Due to the Engram Cycle Collapse, sea winds sometimes converge on the plains to form a spherical barrier that isolates its interior from the outside world. Use [Phantom Dash] near a Wind Barrier to get through it.

6003.Title: Wind Field
Content:
Excerpt 2 from "A Study on Unique Species of Asteria Plains"
Due to the Engram Cycle Collapse, active updrafts occasionally emerge in areas of the ocean-fed plains. Asterians refer to these as [Wind Fields]. While within a Wind Field, use [Multi-Jump] to ascend to a certain height, then use [Float]/[Glide] to soar into the sky on the rising air currents.

6004.Title: Wind Loop
Content:
Excerpt 3 from "A Study on Unique Species of Asteria Plains"
Ring-shaped winds often appear high above Asteria, forming continuous loops. It is hypothesized that these are formed by high concentrations of Winds, condensed in the skies due to the region's unique storm climate. While [Gliding] or [Floating], passing through a [Wind Loop] applies a force perpendicular to the loop's direction, propelling you to its opposite side.

6005.Title: Teleportal
Content: These are teleportation devices left behind by the Bapharian Divines. Once activated, it allows for long-distance teleportation.

6006.Title: Wind Channel
Content:
Excerpt 4 from "A Study on Unique Species of Asteria Plains"
[Wind Channel]: One of the unique phenomena of Asteria. Due to the Engram Cycle Collapse, various elemental turbulence gateways form across the plains. Interact with these small cyclones to open the Wind Channel.
Upon entering a Wind Channel in any posture, you'll be swept up by the turbulence and carried automatically along its path.

6007.Title: Engram Hub
Content: A convergence site of Engram Lines in a region. Adventurers can resonate with it to reestablish a connection to the Engram Lines.
Successful resonance will dispel the fog surrounding the Engram Hub, revealing regional information.
Engram Hubs that have been successfully resonated with also serve as teleportation nodes.

6009.Title: About Illuminating the Journey
Content: Background
Explore around and uncover the beauty and stories of this land. Every journey will become an unforgettable memory.
Introduction

Photo Task: Find the photo spot based on the general location in the album. You can complete the task by interacting with the photo spot and taking a photo while meeting the specified conditions.
Story Quest: Find the people living here and listen to the stories of this place.
Illuminate the Journey: Complete all tasks at a landmark to light it up and claim a reward.

30029.Title: Wish Rules
Content: Wish
Consume Meowlux Wish Coins to make Wishes.
Prizes & Pity

Prizes are divided into four tiers by grade. The rarer the prize, the lower the chance of getting it.
A pity system is in place. If you haven't received an [SSR] after a certain number of wishes, you'll be guaranteed to obtain an [SSR] once the wish count reaches the pity threshold.
Pity Carryover
Pity progress for unclaimed [SSR] and [SR] or higher-tier rewards carries over between Wish Events. Each event maintains independent pity counters
Prize Binding Rules

In each Fashion Wish and Mount Wish event, fashion and accessory boxes, mount boxes, and Meowlux Premium Cards obtained from [SSR] and [SR] are unbound and can be listed in the Trading Center. Items obtained from opening gift boxes will be bound and cannot be listed in the Trading Center.
Items obtained from [R] and [N] are all bound and cannot be listed in the Trading Center.
Additionally, all prizes from each Fashion Wish (Bound) and Mount Wish (Bound) event are bound and cannot be listed in the Trading Center.
Meowlux Premium Card

The prizes obtained from [SSR] and [SR] can be recycled at Kalista in the Meowlux Member Store on the second floor of the shopping center near the Pioneer Bureau for Bound Meowlux Premium Cards.
Meowlux Premium Cards can be used in the Meow Store exchange for various outfits, mounts, mount skins, weapon skins, and more.

30035.Title: Will Wish
Content: Wish
Consume Will Wish Coins to make wishes.
Prizez & Pity

Prizes in the pool are divided into four tiers by grade. The rarer the prize, the lower the chance of drawing it.
A pity system is in place-if you haven't received an [SSR] after a certain number of wishes, you'll be guaranteed to obtain an [SSR] from the current pool once the draw count reaches the pity threshold.
Resonance

All prizes have equal probability of being acquired.
Resonance with a specific Battle Will ensures that the resonated Battle Will is guaranteed upon acquisition of an [SSR].
Resonance Attempts

Resonance available when attempts > 0, acquiring an [SSR] consumes 1 attempt.
Resonance refreshes every Monday at 05:00(UTC-2), can be accumulated up to 1 attempt.
Pity Carryover
Pity progress for unclaimed [SSR] and [SR] or higher-tier rewards carries over between Wish Events.

7000.Title: Guild
Content:
Join a Guild and find fellow adventurers!
Actively communicate with other members in the Guild Channel to stay updated with the latest game information!
The Guild Center unlocks at 05:00(UTC-2) on the second day after server launch. A signature crowdfunding session will then begin, requiring signatures from 10 members. Once the crowdfunding ends, the Guild can begin construction. After the construction is complete, you can use the [Guild Center] button on the Guild homepage to enter the exclusive Guild scene, where convenient facilities and exciting activities await!

Weekly Guild Activity Rewards
After joining a Guild, you can increase your Guild's weekly activity score by contributing personal activity and completing Guild quests. Reach milestones to earn corresponding rewards!
Note: Only personal activity gained on the day you join the Guild will be counted! Features like Check-in, Cargo Delivery, and Hunting Quests will only be available once the Guild Center is fully built!

Guild Construction
Once the Guild Center is unlocked, start by completing the guidance quests given by Receptionist Laura. They will help you understand the roles of each staff and the functions of various facilities in the Guild.
Afterward, you and your fellow members can construct the Guild Center to gain more resources and enjoy additional features!
For example: purchase items from the exclusive Grocery Store, join Guild activities, set photos in the Photo Panel, customize the appearance of your namecard...

Guild Event
In the Guild Event tab, you can view all Guild-exclusive events. The rewards are generous, so make sure to participate actively!

300009.Title: About Commission Attempts
Content:
Each adventurer receives one commission attempt per day. Completing any 3 commissions finishes one round. If an attempt isn't used, it will carry over to the next day (based on server time), up to 3 attempts.
An attempt is consumed as soon as a commission is accepted. If the accepted commission isn't completed by the end of the day, it will be automatically removed on the following day (based on server time).

60010.Title: Storm Particle
Content: You'll find Storm Particles scattered throughout the area. Touching one will trigger a particle collection quest.
Complete the quest within the time limit to earn rewards.

40001.Title: Homestead Commission Overview
Content: Commission
The Traveling Merchant offers 3 commissions daily, requesting specific materials or items. Completing these grants Homestead EXP and Homestead Coins.
Commission Attempts
Up to 3 commissions may be submitted per day, with unused submissions eligible for rollover to subsequent days, not exceeding a maximum of 9 stored submissions.
Commission EXP Cap:
Each week, players can earn up to 190,000 Homestead EXP through Homestead commissions. Once the cap is reached, no additional Homestead EXP can be gained from commissions that week.

40002.Title: Homestead Sale Instructions
Content: Purchase
Use Homestead Coins to purchase items in the Homestead Store.
Sell

Each day, the Shop buys flowers of certain colors and shapes. You can sell your grown flowers to earn Homestead Coins.
The Shop offers two purchase price tiers: premium and standard. Premium-priced flowers have a daily purchase limit.

40003.Title: Homestead Furnitures
Content: Archive
Archive shows all furniture, most of which can be crafted at the Crafting Station or purchased.
Unlock Recipe
Some furniture requires unlocked crafting recipes to be made. Furniture crafting recipes are mainly unlocked through life skills, and the materials required are also sourced from life skills.

40004.Title: Homestead Store
Content: Purchase
You can use Homestead Coins to buy finished furniture and quickly enrich your home.

40005.Title: Homestead Overview
Content: <style="GuideBookLab1">Try Homestead Preview Version!</style>

The initial version of the Homestead System is now live! Adventurers are invited as the first "builders" to unleash creativity in Free Edit Mode. A simple starter layout is provided. All furniture can be freely recycled and rearranged. We look forward to your design ideas!
Feel free to send feedback. Let's create the ideal game home together!
<style="GuideBookLab1">Homestead Upgrade</style>

Home EXP sources:
1. Completing Homestead Commissions
2. Crafting and collecting completed furniture at the Crafting Station
3. Growing Flowers and harvesting mature flowers
Commissions and flower recycling also earn Homestead Coins, which can be used to purchase flower seeds, materials, and furniture in the Homestead Store.
Benefits of Home Upgrades: More plots for flower planting, more slots for furniture crafting, more available purchase options in the Homestead Store, expanded interior space, and broader island activity areas. Most importantly, it increases the furniture placement limit, allowing for even more creativity!
<style="GuideBookLab1">Furniture Acquisition</style>

1. Crafted at Homestead Crafting Station
The primary method of obtaining furniture is to craft it at the homestead crafting station. Place the station wherever most convenient.
Furniture recipes are unlocked via life skills and require materials gathered through them.
Smelting: Unlocks recipes for metal furniture
Artisanry: Unlocks recipes for wood furniture
Gemcrafting: Unlocks recipes for stone furniture
Weaving: Unlocks recipes for fabric/leather furniture
Alchemy: Unlocks recipes for brick/plant furniture
2. Homestead Store Purchase
Use Homestead Coins to buy finished furniture and quickly enrich your home.
<style="GuideBookLab1">Flower Planting</style>

After sowing, flowers go through 3 stages:
1. Growth: Speed up by watering and fertilizing.
2. Pollination:
Manual: Use pollen for breeding.
Passive: If not manually pollinated, flowers auto-cross with neighbors. If none nearby, they inherit seed traits.
3. Harvest: Pick mature flowers.
The rarer the flower, the higher its value! Patience and experimentation are key to breeding rare varieties.
<style="GuideBookLab1">Home Editing & Decoration</style>

1. High-Freedom Editing Mode
Supports 360° furniture rotation and height adjustment with overlapping allowed.
Use [Snap], [Grid], and [Modular] to improve efficiency.
2. Walls
Walls & Floors: They're available at the Homestead Store and support whole house replacement. These are the core to setting the home's tone.
Partition: After placing the partition, you can have wallpapers set by tapping [Wallpaper]. Partition Wallpapers are also purchased from the Homestead Store.
<style="GuideBookLab1">Shared homestead</style>

Reach Friendship Level 2 with a friend to invite them to move in with you. You can level up your homestead, craft furniture, and plant flowers together.
shared homestead does not affect furniture ownership. When shared homestead ends, each player can take back their own Homestead Coins and furniture. Island ownership deeds are preserved for players who had their own home before moving in.
<style="GuideBookLab1">Shared Warehouse</style>
Roommates can place items into the home warehouse for resource exchange. Talk to Storage Manager Sophia to access it.

40006.Title: Homestead Blueprint Guide
Content: Blueprint Introduction
Homestead Blueprints are divided into Interior Blueprints and Courtyard Blueprints. Each blueprint contains decoration data for the corresponding scene.
Creating Blueprints
Adventurers can save their Homestead decoration data as a blueprint. You can also use blueprints shared by other adventurers to quickly replicate their decoration layouts.
Blueprint Share Code
Adventurers can share their saved blueprints with others using a unique Share Code.
Previewing and Applying Blueprints

Adventurers can preview a blueprint to see the decoration effects before deciding to apply it. You must own all furniture in the blueprint to apply it. For furniture you do not currently own, you can quickly purchase it or remove it during the preview to meet the application requirements.
Field
Homestead Fields are the personal assets of the resident. Therefore, applying a blueprint will not change the original field data, nor will the blueprint contain the creator's field data.
Badge Stand & Mannequin
Badges equipped on the badge stand and outfits on the mannequin won't be saved to your Blueprint data. Use them as inspiration to create your own personalized combinations.

20001.Title: Tip
Content:
Enables Forced Lock & Target Switching:
When enabled, the first tap will automatically lock onto the target.
Subsequent taps will cycle through the target list according to priority weights. After cycling through all targets, the forced lock will be released.
Long-pressing while enabled will cancel the forced lock. Moving beyond the forced lock's maximum range will also automatically cancel the lock.

20002.Title: Tip
Content:
Assist Button: Tap the assist button to lock onto a target. Tap again to cancel.
Free Position: Long-press on an empty spot on the screen to lock onto a target. Long-press again to unlock.

30001.Title: About Battle Companions
Content: By resonating with Battle Companions, you can use their abilities and weapons.

30004.Title: About Terra Resonance
Content: With their unique sensory abilities, adventurers resonate with the world's [Engram] to gain special powers.

30005.Title: About Insight Mode
Content: By sensing the Bio-Engram, adventurers activate Insight Mode. While in this mode, they gain deeper knowledge of various lifeforms and can even detect beings normally hidden from view.

501201.Title: View the Mysterious Note
Content: View the note left by Tina.

30023.Title: Base Score Multiplier
Content:
This is the default multiplier for dungeon scores.
Total Score Multiplier = Base Multiplier + Entry Bonus Multiplier
All points you earn in the dungeon will be multiplied by the total score multiplier.

30024.Title: Entry Bonus Multiplier
Content:
This extra multiplier is granted by entries. You can customize entries to change this value.
Total Score Multiplier = Base Multiplier + Entry Bonus Multiplier
All points you earn in the dungeon will be multiplied by the total score multiplier.

30021.Title: Friendship
Content:
Teaming up with friends to clear dungeons, gather items, or explore the world will increase your Friendship.
Note: There is a daily cap on Friendship gained from these activities.
Deleting a friend will reset your Friendship with them to zero.

30031.Title: Time Limit
Content: Defeat monsters and complete events within the time limit to trigger the Boss's arrival. If you fail to meet the conditions in time, the dungeon challenge will end in failure.

30032.Title: Alignment
Content: You can adjust the precision for alignment. When alignment is enabled, movements will adhere to the specified value.

30033.Title: Other Settings
Content: You can adjust the default indoor lighting source.

30101.Title: About World Boss Crusade
Content: Event Time
The World Boss Crusade begins at a fixed time. Players can enter solo or in a team.
Details
Work together to challenge the mighty World Boss and bring them down before time runs out.
Victory Rewards

You can earn point rewards for this event once per day. To qualify, earn at least 1,000 Contribution Points in battles and clear the World Boss Crusade within the time limit. If successful, you'll earn 500 points. If the boss isn't defeated, you'll still earn 300 points for your contribution.
Point Rewards
Earn personal points during battle and accumulate enough points to claim milestone rewards.
World Boss Attributes
In World Boss Crusade, monster HP scales dynamically based on the number of participants, and their attributes grow over time.

30102.Title: About Personal Points
Content: About Points
Defeating the World Boss within the time limit grants basic personal points. Accumulating enough points lets you claim milestone rewards.

20003.Title: Tip
Content:
Enable Auto Combo, and the button of Auto Combo will appear on the action wheel.
Tap the Auto Combo button, and the character will automatically cast skills based on preset logic.
In Auto Combo mode, you can still move, jump, and manually use skills without interrupting the auto combo.

20005.Title: Tip
Content:
When enabled, the camera's facing direction will be considered when selecting a target to lock onto, affecting the final selection.
It's recommended that ranged classes keep this setting enabled.

20006.Title: Tip
Content: Camera Shake: Caused by the character's skills, resulting in camera movement centered on the character.

20007.Title: Tip
Content: Pulse Shake: Caused by external vibration sources, usually triggered by Elite or Boss monsters.

20008.Title: Tip
Content:
When enabled, enters Auto Combo Mode. This feature can be activated via a hotkey,
allowing the character to cast skills based on a preset logic.
In Auto Combo mode, you can still move, jump, and manually use skills without interrupting the auto combo.

20009.Title: Tip
Content:
Access the Auto Combo Settings screen.
Tap skill icons to toggle their availability in auto-combos.
If a skill is swapped out in a slot, the toggle state will carry over to the new skill.
These settings only affect auto-combo mode and do not impact manual skill use.

20012.Title: Tip
Content:
Free Cast Mode enables directional targeting for some skills via a wheel.
Hold the skill button to bring up the casting wheel.
Release to cast at the chosen direction and range.
This mode is disabled while using auto-combos.

20013.Title: Tip
Content:
Free Cast Mode enables full positional control for some skills.
Hold the skill to bring up a target indicator. Use your mouse to move it.
The skill will then be cast toward the marker location.
Character movement is unrestricted during targeting. Right-click to cancel the cast.
Free Casting Mode does not apply when Auto Combo is enabled.

20014.Title: Tip
Content: Skill Camera Movement: During skill casting, the camera may move and the field of view (FOV) may change, adding a more dynamic, action-oriented feel.

20015.Title: Tip
Content: While strafing, the camera will automatically rotate in the direction of movement.

20016.Title: Tip
Content: If the camera distance is too close when casting a skill, it will automatically zoom out to a more suitable view.

20017.Title: Tip
Content: If there is a large angle difference during skill casting, the camera will automatically adjust its orientation to a more optimal position.

20018.Title: Tip
Content: If your view is too close when using a skill on a locked-on target, the camera will automatically zoom out to a better distance.

20019.Title: Tip
Content: Upon entering combat, the maximum camera zoom-out distance will increase, and revert to normal when leaving combat.

20021.Title: Tips:
Content: After enabling, a golden line appears to guide players to the target NPC when near.

20022.Title: Tips:
Content:
In "Release to Cast" mode, press a skill key to show the indicator, and release the key to cast.
In "Left-Click" mode, press a skill key to show the indicator, then left-click or press the skill key again to cast.
In both modes, right-click cancels the cast.

20020.Title: Tips:
Content: When enabled, mouse movement outside the game window is allowed in windowed mode.

20023.Title: Tip
Content: When enabled, the camera will glide smoothly when swiping, gradually slowing down before stopping.

20024.Title: Tip
Content:
You can adjust character voice volume via: Settings - Basic - Audio Settings - Game Voice.
Switching voice profiles does not consume any items.

30034.Title: No Time Limit
Content: There is no time limit for the dungeon at this level.

30002.Title: About Mails
Content: Mail Content

The mailbox can hold up to 500 mails.
When this limit is exceeded, the system will automatically delete older mails without attachments or with already claimed attachments, starting from the oldest.
When the number of unclaimed mails with attachments reaches 100, a pop-up window will prompt you to claim them all at once.
When the number of read mails reaches 40, a pop-up window will prompt you to delete them all at once.
Favorites Explanation

Mails categorized under [Bookmark] cannot be deleted using [Delete All].
You can unfavor the mails or delete them manually. Deleted mails cannot be recovered.
Mail Expiry
Each mail has its own validity period. Once expired, the mail will be removed from the mailbox regardless of whether it has attachments.

30003.Title: Guild Introduction
Content: Guild Center Unlocked

The Guild Center unlocks at 05:00(UTC-2) on the second day after server launch. A signature crowdfunding session will then begin, requiring signatures from 10 members. Once the crowdfunding ends, the Guild can begin construction. After the construction is complete, you can use the [Guild Center] button on the Guild homepage to enter the exclusive Guild scene, where convenient facilities and exciting activities await!
Guild Introduction

Guild Members
-The initial member limit is 80. When the Guild Recreation reaches Lv.6, the cap increases to 120 members.
Guild Channel
-Engage with other members in the Guild channel to stay updated with the latest game info!
Guild Development
-Development requires Guild Funds, which can be earned by completing Guild-related quests and events.
Guild Management
-Some Guild settings require specific permissions. Authorized members can adjust settings for features such as Guild Cover, Guild Album Review, Guild Level-up, Photo Panel, and the Guild Boon Fountain. Non-authorized members can view these settings only.
Weekly Guild Activity Rewards

After joining a Guild, you can increase your Guild's weekly activity score by contributing personal activity and completing Guild quests. Reach milestones to earn corresponding rewards!
Note: Only personal activity gained on the day you join the Guild will be counted! Features like Check-in, Cargo Delivery, and Hunting Quests will only be available once the Guild Center is fully built!
Guild Construction

Once the Guild Center is unlocked, start by completing the guidance quests given by Receptionist Laura. They will help you understand the roles of each staff and the functions of various facilities in the Guild.
Afterward, you and your fellow members can construct the Guild Center to gain more resources and enjoy additional features!
For example: purchase items from the exclusive Grocery Store, join Guild activities, set photos in the Photo Panel, customize the appearance of your namecard...
Guild Activities
In the Guild Event tab, you can view all Guild-exclusive events. The rewards are generous, so make sure to participate actively!

30006.Title: Engram Breakpoint
Content: Each region contains several locations where the flow of Engram is obstructed, preventing the formation of a complete Engram Line cycle-these are known as Engram Breakpoints. Once adventurers establish a connection with the Engram Hub, they can spot these Breakpoints and resonate with them to help restore the Engram Linecycle for the Engram Hub in the region.

30007.Title: About Adventurer Level
Content: About Adventurer Levels

1. You can gain EXP by completing Daily Activity Goals, Quests, and defeating monsters. Earn enough EXP to reach higher Adventurer Levels.
2. Increasing your Adventurer Level not only grants attribute boosts, Bio-Engram Gifts, and Talent Points, but also unlocks additional features.
Note: Max Level for This Version: Level 60.
Unclaimed EXP

1. Starting from the first day of each season, any Unclaimed EXP from daily Activity Chests will accumulate in the Unclaimed EXP Pool, including EXP from days before account creation.
2. Players will receive an equal amount of Unclaimed EXP whenever they gain EXP in-game until the Unclaimed EXP Pool is depleted.
Newbie Explanation

Newbie Criteria: On Server Day 5, the system will conduct a newbie check, marking all players ≤Lv.29 with a Newbie tag. The Newbie tag will be automatically removed from any player after 28 days of real time.
Newbie Perks: When players with the Newbie tag participate in Unstable Space, Chaotic Realm, Guild Hunt, Stimen Vaults, Chaotic Realm Master, and 20-player raid, Newbie players gain extra Friendship Points. Weekly caps apply.
Assisting Newbie: Teaming with Newbie players in Chaotic Realm, Guild Hunt, Stimen Vaults, Chaotic Realm Master, and 20-player raid will also grant assisting players extra Friendship Points. Weekly caps apply.

30030.Title: Stellar Memory
Content: Background
Fragments of memories from various lifeforms on this planet linger here. If adventurers explore these Memory Realms, they might even recover pieces of their own lost memories.
Introduction

1. You can enter Memory Realms solo or with a partner.
2. Each Memory Realm offers unique challenges, and some may contain special anomalies that provide either positive or debuffs.
3. You must complete one realm's trial to unlock access to the next. Some Memory Realms require a countdown to finish before they become accessible.
4. Each challenge attempt grants immediate rewards, and as you accumulate Exploration, you can claim bonus rewards in the [Challenge Log].
5. Reaching the recommended gear level and using proper battle strategies can help you overcome the challenges-don't let the eerie memories scare you off, adventurer!

400001.Title: About Battle Companions
Content: Background

Due to their unique Bio-Engram, players can form Resonance with designated Battle Companions. The Companion's power manifests as Imaginative Force, staying by the player's side. When battle begins, players can wield their weapons directly.
Introduction

1) You can resonate with up to two Battle Companions at once. Tap the "Switch" button on the Companion screen or the avatar on the main interface to swap weapons.
2) Up to 3 Resonance Loadouts can be pre-set and freely switched based on different combat environments.
3) Tap on any Battle Companion to view details or change to a different one.
Guardian Activation

Once two Battle Companions are resonated, a Guardian Buff can be activated, granting bonus effects.
For more details, please refer to the "Guardian" system tutorial.

400002.Title: Talent
Content: Talent
Each Class has its own unique set of Talents, which automatically switches when the player changes classes.
Talent Development

Each class features a set of talent nodes, including stat boosts, passive effects, and unique skill modifiers. Players can activate nodes by spending Talent PTS.
Talents are divided into two stages. The second stage unlocks only after meeting certain allocation conditions in the first. A playstyle path must be selected before continuing in the second stage.
Strategic talent combinations allow players to craft a customized combat style.
Other Features

Recommended Allocation: Use this feature to quickly assign Talent Points based on system-suggested builds, allowing you to efficiently activate each phase of your Talent Nodes.
Reset: If you're not satisfied with your current allocation, you can tap the Reset button to redistribute your Talent Points. All previously used points and materials will be fully refunded upon reset.
Special Note on Shared Talent Items

Talent Points Sharing: All Talent Points used in the Talent System are shared across all loadouts.
"Arcane Seals" Sharing When allocating Talent Points, Arcane Seals consumed are stored in an Arcane Seal Container. (Attention: Only Arcane Seals spent when unlocking talents are deposited into the container.) Once stored, all Talent Loadouts share this total value. Example: If you spend "Arcane Seal - Attack" x10 while unlocking talents in Loadout Page 1, all 10 Seals are placed into the "Arcane Seal - Attack" Container. When switching to another loadout and unlocking talents requiring the same type, the system will deduct those 10 Seals in that Container first. Only after the Container is empty will new "Arcane Seal - Attack" Seals be consumed and added to the Container.
Attention: Any Arcane Seals added into the Container cannot be returned to your inventory. They exist solely for Talent System sharing and cannot be withdrawn.

30008.Title: Anchor Notes
Content: Investigation Themes
Complete specific quests while exploring the overworld to unlock Investigation Themes.
Puzzles
Use [Puzzle] to enter the deduction interface tied to the investigation theme.
Clues
While exploring and speaking with others, pay attention-some casually dropped hints might reveal the world's hidden truths.
Deduction
Follow logical reasoning to identify key clues that lead you toward the truth.
Conclusion
Once all deductions are complete, the final conclusion of the investigation will be revealed.
Quick Deduction
Tap [Quick Deduction] to skip the reasoning process and instantly obtain a result.

30009.Title: Anchor Notes
Content: Clues
Explore the world and talk to different people-hidden truths may be revealed in their casual remarks.
Deduction
Based on the clues you've gathered so far,

30010.Title: Time Machine
Content: Background

Formidable foes and heart-pounding battles now unfold vividly before your eyes. To continue your journey of exploration and conquest across this vast land, step forward-into the surging tide of memory.
Introduction

Once you've completed a main story dungeon, its Time Machine becomes available-an expanded challenge filled with hidden content. While the main clear reward is one-time only, you're free to revisit to hunt for secrets, complete missed objectives, and unlock milestone rewards based on your Exploration.
Monster Difficulty
Monster attributes in this mode dynamically adjust based on your power rating. HP is balanced for Guardian and Support classes, making solo exploration easy and accessible.

30011.Title: "Unit X" Investigation-Unstable Space
Content: Background

The "Unit X" is a large, unidentified mechanical device. Investigations reveal that it differs from known teleportation technologies, and its function remains unclear. The Spatiotemporal Investigation Unit under the Bapharian Creed has officially authorized the Pioneer Bureau to jointly investigate the device and is recruiting adventurers from across the continent to participate.
Introduction

Unstable Space: The central entrance beneath the machine leads to a special dungeon. The dungeon and its entries will vary each day. Adventurers are required to investigate the new conditions each day.
Monster Attribute

Unstable Space is a light dungeon designed for resource acquisition. Monsters within feature dynamic balancing. Their attributes and HP scale based on the number of participants and power rating, ensuring a better experience for all adventurers.
Rewards

The rewards from Unstable Space improve as the server progresses. At launch, it drops Lv.220 gear and Reforge Stones. When a new gear level becomes available, it will begin dropping gear and Reforge Stones of that level, along with materials used to forge exclusive weapons. These materials have a fixed drop rate of 1 piece every 2 clears. You can check the reward preview in Unstable Space for details. Missed rewards can be reclaimed the following week via the Reclaim Hub.

Both Solo/Duo and Multiplayer modes of Unstable Space now drop the same items, so you may freely choose either mode.

30012.Title: "Unit X" Investigation-Chaotic Realm Introduction
Content: Background

The "Unit X" is a large, unidentified mechanical device. Investigations reveal that it differs from known teleportation technologies, and its function remains unclear. The Spatiotemporal Investigation Unit under the Bapharian Creed has officially authorized the Pioneer Bureau to jointly investigate the device and is recruiting adventurers from across the continent to participate.
Core Gameplay
Chaotic Realm Challenge: The top of the device features six sets of interactive mechanism platforms. When adventurers step onto them, they can see the target stage corresponding to the area.
Difficulty

Each stage has three difficulty tiers:
- Normal: An entry-level experience with solo and duo options, allowing team-ups with AI companions.

- Hard: An advanced challenge with limited revives at the start. 5 days after the dungeon opens, players who die during the boss battle can revive via countdown or Ally assistance.

- Master: An extreme challenge. Each cleared stage unlocks a higher difficulty. New difficulty levels open as the server time progresses. The higher the difficulty, the stronger the entries and monsters. Clearing higher difficulties faster increases your maximum score per dungeon. Raising your total Master Mode score earns exclusive seasonal glory rewards.

Note: Hard and Master modes include a "Boss Activation Countdown." Players must complete specific objectives within the time limit to unlock the boss battle. Failing to do so results in challenge failure.

Note: Deaths in the dungeon will additionally reduce the remaining time. Plan your survival strategy carefully.

Solo/Dual Mode unlocks for Hard Mode, Master 1, and Master VI after the dungeon has been available for a certain number of days. Players can team up with AI companions. During boss challenges, fallen players can revive via countdown or teammate assistance. Unlock timing varies by difficulty, but rewards remain unchanged in Solo/Dual Mode.
Rewards

First Clear Rewards:
Normal/Hard/Master 1/Master 6: Legendary gear select chests of the corresponding levels.

Clear Rewards: Each successful clear of Hard or higher difficulty grants a piece of Legendary gear for the current class.

Special Event Rewards: If the "Squeaky Boarlet" event appears in the dungeon, successfully defeating the Squeaky Boarlet and interacting with the devices it leaves behind before clearing the dungeon grants special loot rewards (weekly limit of 5, counted separately across Normal + Hard, Master 1–5, and Master 6+ pools).
Dungeon-Exclusive Appearance Acquisition

1. Dreamvoid Dust, the general exchange material for the exclusive dungeon appearances of the "Echoes of Ember" season: Drops in all difficulties.
Drop limit: Normal/Hard: 800 total; Master 1–5: 800 total; Master 6 and above: no limit.

2. Dreamvoid Silk, the special exchange material for the exclusive dungeon appearances of the "Echoes of Ember" season: Each dungeon drops its own corresponding type of Dreamvoid Silk.
Drop Limit: Each type of Dreamvoid Silk has a total drop limit of 100 across all difficulties in its respective dungeon.
Monster Attribute Notes

In Chaotic Realm (Normal), monsters' HP scales dynamically based on the number of participating players. Power Scores do not affect monsters' HP or other attributes. In Chaotic Realm (Hard & Master), monsters' HP and other attributes are not affected by the number of participating players or Power Scores.
About Reforge Stone

Normal/Hard modes drop Reforge Stone Lv.220.
Master 1–5 drop Reforge Stone Lv.240.
Master 6 and above drop Reforge Stone Lv.260.
Reforge Stone rewards have a weekly limit. Limits for different levels of Reforge Stones are independent.

30013.Title: Gear
Content: Gear

Adventurers can enhance their combat strength by equipping gear.
Gear Slot: There are 11 equipment slots in total: Weapon, Helmet, Armor, Gauntlets, Boots, Necklace, Earrings, Ring, Charm, Bracelet (L), and Bracelet (R).
Gear Effect: Gear effects are categorized into Basic Attributes, Advanced Attributes (which include Reforged Attribute and special Rare Attribute), and Cultivation Effects (such as refinement, embedding, and set effects, etc).
Equip and Unequip Gear: Adventurers can equip, unequip, and replace gear through the Gear interface. Choose gear that suits your class/style.
Gear Enhancement: At the Artisan Guild, players can reforge, refine, embed, or dismantle gear to enhance its power.
Tips: Some Legendary Gear will auto-adapt based on your current style. It is recommended to prioritize equipping such gear when available.
Reforge Function

The first reforge unlocks a reforging attribute under Advanced Attributes, while subsequent reforges will steadily improve the gear's perfection level. For gear below the max perfection level, Reforge enhances basic and advanced attributes. However, once perfection is capped, it can no longer be further reforged. At that point, if the current reforging attribute is unsatisfactory, the modification feature can be used to manually select an attribute from that gear's reforging effect pool to apply. Notably, when reforged gear is dismantled, reforge materials will be refunded, making it risk-free to reforge pre-capped gear.
Refine Function

Refine may succeed or fail, but failure will not reduce the level. Blessing items can increase the refining success rate. When you replace gear of the same slot, refinement levels are automatically transferred for free.
Embedding

Embedding a gem/sigil into gear will transfer its attributes onto the gear. Intermediate or advanced embedding grants higher-level gem/sigil effects. (Note: Gems/sigils embedded in the gear will not be returned upon dismantling.)
Modification Feature

Modification is divided into Reforge Effect Modification and Rare Effect Modification.
Reforge Effect Modification: When gear reaches maximum Perfection, it can no longer be reforged. If the current reforge effects are unsatisfactory, one effect can be manually selected from the gear's reforge effect pool.
Rare Effect Modification: If the current rare effect is unsatisfactory, one effect can be manually selected from the gear's rare effect pool. Some gear does not initially have a rare effect, but one can still be selected from the gear's rare effect pool through Modification.
Note: 1) The Modification interface only displays gear available for the current class. Gear for other classes will only appear after switching to that class. 2) Currently, for specialization-adaptive gear, only certain special weapons can be modified. Modification results are calculated independently for each specialization. 3) Only one effect can be selected per Modification. Each Modification consumes materials separately, and consumed materials are not refunded upon dismantling. Choose entry effects carefully.
Outfit Set

Some gear above Lv.60 includes a set effect that adapts to the Spec, allowing for flexible selection. (Tips: Set effects apply to up to 4 pieces. Adventurers can freely choose any 4 slots to equip. Since set attributes adapt to your current Spec, it's recommended to prioritize equipping pieces that don't match your current attributes. Both 2-piece and 4-piece set effects can be activated and will take effect simultaneously. Lv.80 set gear can be worn together with Lv.60 gear to trigger set effect without change.) Set can be dismantled, and exchange and reforging materials will be fully refunded. (Note: Embedded gems cannot be recovered upon dismantling.)
Dismantling
Dismantle unwanted gear to get materials. If the gear was reforged, the used materials will be fully refunded.(Note: You can dismantle up to 300 items for a singular dismantling.)
Perfection

Perfection is a straightforward indicator of gear's quality, with a maximum value of 100.
Perfection Cap: Different pieces of gear may have different Perfection caps. Before the gear's Perfection reaches the cap, each Reforge will change (increase) the Perfection. However, once the gear's Perfection reaches the cap, it can no longer be reforged.

30015.Title: Drink Mixing
Content: Drink Mixing
Mix drinks based on the customer's request!
Drink Recipes
Tap the [Drink Recipes] button in the lower-left corner to view available recipes.
Start Mixing

Once you've found the requested recipe, tap the ingredient icons on the right to add base liquids and supplementary ingredients to the pot.
Each tap adds one unit of the selected ingredient.
Some drinks require a specific Preparation Method-only one method can be selected per mix. If a recipe doesn't mention a method, it means no method is required.
After adding all ingredients, tap [Mix] to begin mixing.
Redo & Skip

Messed up? Just tap the [Redo] button in the lower-left corner to start over!
No idea what to make? Use the [Skip] button in the lower-left corner to instantly serve the correct drink.

300014.Title: About Terra Resonance
Content: Background

As an extraordinary Adventurer, you are able to resonate with certain creatures in the environment, temporarily absorbing their innate traits and abilities. These powers can be stored within your body and freely controlled at will.
By adapting to different terrains and environmental conditions, you can make strategic use of these resonance-based abilities to explore the continent more effectively.
Introduction

Float & Glide: By summoning Hero's Herb, it is as if you've grown a pair of wings, awakening the imagination to soar in the sky.
When you are at a certain height above the ground, you can enter a floating state and slowly fall. The joystick can change the direction of the fall.
While floating, you can switch to gliding mode to travel through the air at higher speeds. The joystick can change the direction and angle of gliding.
Phantom Dash: The Wind Core, born from the hurricanes on the plains, seems to possess the ability to withstand storms. By summoning Wind Core, it seems to awaken the imagination that can break through all obstacles.
The first dash propels you forward a short distance and knocks back monsters in your path. Using it again within a certain timeframe will trigger a second dash with the same effect as the first.
Depending on when the second dash is used, it will also quickly restore a certain amount of summoning energy.

30016.Title: Potential Activation
Content: Background
As an extraordinary adventurer, you can unlock greater physical potential as your strength increases—enhancing your mobility and your ability to overcome environmental obstacles.
Introduction

Once your Adventurer Level reaches certain stages, you'll be able to freely select and configure potential builds. Some levels unlock multiple options for you to choose from.
Each potential is designed for specific scenarios, but activation requires no manual input from you.
For example: when the "Wall Run" potential is activated, you'll be able to seamlessly traverse vertical surfaces such as high walls or cliff faces.
As your Adventurer Level grows, more potentials become available. You can mix and match them freely to develop your own unique movement style.

30018.Title: World Event
Content: Background

Across this land, where Engram surges endlessly, anomalies have begun to surface due to disruptions in the Engram Line flow. Take on world event challenges and confront these disturbances-doing so may lead to unexpected rewards...
Introduction

Before a World Event begins, you can tap the special icon on the map to preview the event and check its countdown.
World Events are categorized by complexity and difficulty into Blue, Purple, and Orange tiers. Completing them grants 1, 2, or 3 Pioneer Medals respectively. Pioneer Medal can be exchanged for daily rewards.
There is a daily limit on how many Pioneer Medals you can earn, and event rewards can accumulate for up to five days.

30020.Title: Monster Hunt
Content: Introduction

The wildness is home to various types of monsters. Explore the area and engage in battles to complete Monster Hunt objectives and earn rewards to upgrade your Monster Manual.
In addition to monsters, you'll encounter powerful Elite and Boss monsters in the wildness. Defeating them drops Loots that can be unlocked with keys to obtain rare rewards. Elite Keys and Boss Keys can be obtained daily at 05:00(UTC-2), 2 each per day. When the quantity held exceeds 6, no more will be automatically granted (tracked separately per key type).
Each time you defeat an Elite or Boss, you'll gain EXP, Season Points, and Luno, along with a chance to obtain rare monster materials. Bosses also have a chance to drop gear.
Elites and bosses also drop Loots. Elite chests can only be opened by the player or party who first dealt DMG. Boss chests can be opened by anyone who dealt DMG to the boss, healed other players, or took DMG from the boss. Use the corresponding loot key to open them and claim your rewards, even including modules and rare materials. For full details, check the Reward Preview in the Monster Manual. Excess key rewards will be recoverable next week via the Reclaim Hub.
Additionally, magical creatures appear in the wildness at random times. Defeating them grants headwear, mount skins, toys, and other rewards. Check the Magical Creature tab for more info.
Monster Attributes

HP of field elites and magical creatures scales with the number of players present.
Boss attributes and HP dynamically adjust based on both player count and power rating. Generally, the more players involved, the more efficient the hunt.

10011.Title: Weapon
Content: Weapon Slots
You have two weapon slots, allowing you to equip and switch between two weapons during combat to adapt to different situations.

10012.Title: Weapon Enhancement
Content: Enhancement
Use specific items to enhance your weapon level and increase its base stats.
Transformation
Through modification, you can upgrade weapon skill levels, enhance skill effects, or replace skills entirely.

30022.Title: Total friendship
Content: Total Friendship

Your Total Friendship is the sum of all Friendship Points gained with friends. Deleting friends won't reduce the total. Teaming up to complete dungeons, collect items, or explore the world increases this total.
There is a daily cap on how many points you can earn-any points beyond the cap won't be counted.

10013.Title: Weapon Forging
Content: Forging
Use specific materials to forge designated weapons.

400003.Title: Module
Content: Module Introduction

Modules are advanced technologies inherited from a previous era. Widely equipped by adventurers, modules offer powerful performance boosts in battle. They come in three quality tiers: Basic, Advanced, and Excellent, with Excellent modules providing the strongest effects. Each adventurer can equip up to five modules at a time.
Effect

Each module contains Power Cores: Basic modules carry 1 core, Advanced modules carry 2 cores, and Excellent modules carry 3 cores. For equipped modules, every successful link can increase the effectiveness. In addition, the system will track the total number of successful links across all equipped modules for each type of Power Core. When the total successful link count reaches certain thresholds, you'll unlock that Power Core's Lv.1-6 effects accordingly.
Level of Power Cores

Each Power Cores is assigned a random Link Level when acquired. The distribution varies by module type:
1. Basic Modules – Contain 1 Power Core effect. Link Level ranges from +1 to +10.
2. Advanced Modules – Contain 2 Power Core effects. Each Link Level ranges from +1 to +10.
3. Excellent Modules – Contain 3 Power Core effects. First and second effects: Link Level ranges from +1 to +10; Third effect: Link Level ranges from +1 to +5.
Tips: For detailed info about Modules, adventurers can visit the official website.
Module Dismantling
Extra modules can be dismantled. Once dismantled, the module will disappear, and you'll receive special materials in return. Note: You can dismantle up to 300 items for a singular dismantling.

30050.Title: Quest Types
Content: Story

In a moment both ordinary and precious, the adventurer set out on a journey with their companions. As they pursued the Void Engram and lost memories, little did they know-the fate of The Continent of Magna was already shifting.
World Quests
Explore the mysterious The Continent of Magna from a new perspective.
Map Events
During your journey across The Continent of Magna, you'll encounter fascinating people, strange occurrences, and sudden events. Don't miss the chance to take part when they appear!
Guide Quests
The Continent of Magna is full of unique experiences waiting for adventurers to discover.
Event Quests
Let's have fun together during the event!

30025.Title: Season Store
Content: Season Store

During each season, you can use Season Points in the shop to purchase various items, including upgrade materials and outfit coloring vouchers.
Participating in seasonal activities across different game modes earns you a large number of Season Points!
Shop items vary by season, so be sure to spend your points before the season ends!

30026.Title: Pioneer Manual
Content: Manual Objectives
Follow the objectives outlined in the Manual to quickly learn essential adventuring techniques.
Preview & Claim Rewards
Completing each objective lets you claim a reward. The manual contains content for five days. Objectives can be completed early, but rewards will only be available on or after their scheduled date.

30027.Title: Features
Content: Features
Here you can view the activities available to you. Check out their descriptions and rewards, then choose your favorite and jump in!
Tag

Daily tags indicate activities that can be completed every day. Weekly tags mean the rewards reset weekly. Seasonal tags show that the rewards can only be obtained once per season. Leisure tags mark non-combat, relaxed daily activities. Exploration tags require players to actively explore the world. Event tags indicate recurring, time-limited activities.

30040.Title: Wardrobe
Content: Wardrobe

Appearance Display: Both gear and outfits can change your character's look. When both are equipped on the same slot, the outfit's appearance will be displayed by default.
Visibility Settings: You can toggle the visibility of appearances via the [Settings] option in the Wardrobe.
Dyeing: Collect dye items to recolor your outfits-each color change consumes one dye item.

30060.Title: Hunting
Content: Hunting

Players can team up with other guild members to enter either Easy or Hard hunting dungeons. Completing a hunt grants rewards and Hunting Points. When the total Hunting Points of the guild reach a certain threshold, all guild members will receive bonus rewards.
Difficulty

Normal: Designed for all guild members. Monster attributes and HP scale with power rating and player count, ensuring accessibility for solo and team play.

Hard: Offers a greater challenge. Monster attributes grow over time, and HP scales with player count. Cooperation with your guild members is highly recommended!

30061.Title: Leaderboard
Content: Hunting Vanguard Leaderboard

Players earn points by completing hunts or assisting others. At the end of the event, the top 5 point earners will receive the Hunting Vanguard title, which appears next to their name in guild chat as a mark of honor.

30062.Title: Information Description
Content: Information Description
The information interface is used to display the basic information of the character, including: avatar, nickname, Class, talent style, ability score, Adventurer Level, attribute details, etc.
Avatar Area

Tap the avatar to enter the profile to set up your character card and basic information. Tap the ability score to enter the interface to view your score details. Tap the Adventurer Level to enter the interface to view your Adventurer Level details.
Class and Style
Tap the Class area to enter the interface to choose and switch your Class. Tap the talent to enter the interface to view your talent situation or switch specs.
Basic Attributes
This interface only displays important attributes. Tap on attribute details to view all attribute information.

90009.Title: Title 1 of Group 1
Content: Test Content

400102.Title: Battle Imagines
Content: Battle Imagine

By using a Battle Imagine item, adventurers can acquire and activate the unique combat abilities of other lifeforms-known as Battle Imagine skills. These are powerful combat support abilities.
Each Battle Imagine skill includes one active effect and at least one passive effect.
Battle Imagine skills come in various types based on your main attributes and spec. Adventurers can select Battle Imagine skills that match their spec and role in combat, whether focused on offense, defense, or support.
Activation & Equipment

Battle Imagine must be activated before they can be equipped, which consumes the corresponding Battle Imagine.
Adventurers can activate and collect multiple Battle Imagine skills, but may only equip two at a time. Choose your equipment strategically based on your class and combat environment to maximize your effectiveness.
Battle Imagine skills can also be upgraded, enhancing both active and passive effects, and may unlock additional bonus effects over time.
Battle Imagines

A Battle Imagine is a crystallized fusion of thought, Will, and Engram, imbued with supernatural power. Their mysterious origin traces back to the Bapharian civilization and remains unsolved to this day. Due to their complexity, they cannot be mass-produced artificially.
Crafting Battle Imagines

Battle Imagines required for activation and upgrading can be crafted. Crafting a Battle Imagine consumes the corresponding Battle Wills along with other materials, which can be obtained by defeating monsters or participating in various game activities.
Dismantling Battle Imagines

Excess "Battle Imagine" and "Battle Will" items can be dismantled to obtain special materials, which can then be exchanged for other items. (Note: You can dismantle up to 300 items for a singular Imagine and Will dismantling.)

400103.Title: Crafting Battle Imagines
Content: Crafting Battle Imagines

Battle Imagines can be crafted. Crafting a Battle Imagine consumes the corresponding Battle Wills along with other materials.
About Battle Will: Each Battle Imagine requires a unique type of Battle Will. This is a core material essential for crafting the Imagine.
About Other Materials: Additional materials needed for crafting can be obtained by defeating monsters or participating in various game activities.

400104.Title: Dismantling Battle Imagines
Content: Dismantling Battle Imagines

Excess Battle Imagine items can be dismantled. Dismantled Battle Imagines disappear and grant materials.
Materials obtained from dismantling Battle Imagines can be exchanged for other desired Battle Imagine items.
(Note: The total number of Imagines and Wills dismantled in a single action cannot exceed 300.)

400106.Title: Breakthrough Overview
Content: Breakthrough Overview

Some unique gear can undergo Breakthrough, further raising its level and enhancing its effects.
Each piece of gear has a season‑specific Breakthrough cap. Once it reaches the maximum gear level for that item/season, it can no longer be upgraded through Breakthrough.
If you dismantle gear that has undergone Breakthrough, the materials spent on Breakthrough will be returned.

400107.Title: Gear Crafting
Content: Gear Crafting

Some unique gear can be crafted. This kind of gear always has 100 Perfection and outperforms regular ones!
Special Note on the "Far Sea" series weapons: Crafting a Lv.100 "Far Sea" weapon requires 100 Glinting Stellar Cores and 250 Thrones (Pure Gold). Further on, the "Far Sea" weapons can be further upgraded to higher levels through breakthrough.
Craft Details
Typically, you need to consume one lower-grade rare item with 100 Perfection, along with some special materials.
Note:

1. Specific gear may be required for crafting.
2. Crafted gear has fixed attributes, regardless of materials used.

400108.Title: Reclaim Hub Overview
Content: Reclaim Hub Overview
Missed certain challenges or rewards? You can redeem some items in the Reclaim Hub using Reclaim Tokens, though some value may be lost.
Reclaim Token
If you don't have enough points to redeem all of the week's compensatory rewards, you'll earn bonus Reclaim Tokens proportionally when earning Honor Coins through activities.
Reclaim Items Overview

The Reclaim Hub dynamically updates available items and your Reclaim Tokens based on your activity and rewards earned.
Currently, the modules affecting Reclaim Items include, but are not limited to: Daily activity pack claims, daily Focus acquisition, excess Elite and Boss chest keys, number of entries into Unstable Space, highest floor cleared in Stimen Vaults during the current cycle, weekly progress and rewards earned in Dragon Shackles...
Additional Notes

Note 1: The weekly cap on Reclaim Tokens resets every Monday at 05:00(UTC-2).
Note 2: Each item in the Reclaim Hub has a stock limit.
Note 3: Compensation is issued based on the current week of the season.
Note 4: Some items in the Reclaim Hub will be cleared during cross-season resets.

400109.Title: Role Skills
Content: Skills

Role Skills are divided into three types based on their battlefield function: Attack, Survival, and Support.
Each class can only equip its corresponding Role Skills. If you want to use skills from another role, you must first change class.
Equipment

The skills for each role can be freely equipped.
In the Role Skills interface, select the Role Skill you want to equip and use the Equip button to enter the setup screen. There, you can equip your desired skills to the skill slots by dragging them into place.

90010.Title: Title 2 of Group 1
Content: Test Content

90011.Title: Title 3 of Group 2
Content: Test Content

90012.Title: Title 4 of Group 2
Content: Test Content

10014.Title: Elemental Countering
Content:
Monsters have corresponding elemental defense attributes, and player weapons have corresponding elemental attack attributes.
When the attack attribute restrains the defense attribute, DMG dealt +25%.
When the attack attribute is restrained by the defense attribute, DMG dealt -25%.

Note: Player defense attributes are Neutral by default. Neutral attributes are not restrained by any other attributes.

10015.Title: Carnival Event
Content: Carnival Event
Here you can view all Carnival activities and their weekly schedule. Pick your favorite events and join in!

400004.Title: Season Titles
Content: Season Titles

Season Titles are divided into multiple tiers, ranked from low to high. Each title is further divided into several star levels. Upon reaching a designated title, players will receive not only basic rewards, but also visual rewards such as outfits and emblems. At the same time, advancing your Season Title may also be a prerequisite for unlocking and developing season-related progression modules.
Title Promotion
Complete Seasonal Achievements to earn Gold and Silver Trophies. Once you have enough, you will be able to upgrade your Title.
Title Reset

Since each season has a different theme, the corresponding season titles and title rewards also vary. At the start of a new season, season titles will be reset, and all related seasonal items will become invalid.

400005.Title: Season Achievements
Content: Season Achievement Introduction

Achievement quests are designed based on the seasonal content, gameplay focus, and themes. They span combat, exploration, character progression, and social interaction, offering players fresh and challenging goals every season. Achievements will be fully updated each season. Take on the challenges and pursue new glories every time!

400101.Title: Class Skill
Content: Skills

Class Skills include 3 fixed skills and several Select Expertise.
Each set of Class Skills corresponds to a specific class, and adventurers can only use the skills of their class. To use skills from another class, you must first switch to that class.
Additionally, in order to cast Class Skills, you must equip a weapon that matches the corresponding class.
Equipment

Each class has 3 fixed skills that cannot be freely configured and are automatically assigned to designated skill slots. In addition, you can freely choose and equip 4 Select Expertise.
In the Class Skill interface, select the Expertise Skill you want to equip and use the Equip button to enter the setup screen. There, you can equip your desired skills to the skill slots by dragging them into place.
Skill Training

Skills can be enhanced through Leveling Up and Advancement.
Skill Level-Up: Increases the skill's additional DMG.
Skill Advancement: May increase the skill's DMG multiplier or grant additional effects. Adventurers can view details in the Skill Advancement interface.

30028.Title: Activity and Passes
Content: Activity

Earn activity points by completing daily objectives. Accumulate enough points to claim daily rewards.
Some objectives grant bonus activities when completed. Prioritize these to reach your daily goal faster.
You can manually refresh objectives once per day. Refreshing will randomly change the multiplier of unfinished objectives. If all bonus objectives for the day have already been completed, refreshing will no longer generate any new ones.
Some objectives can be repeated, allowing you to earn activities multiple times from the same task.
Activity progress resets daily at 05:00.
Season Pass

By accumulating Season EXP, you can increase your Season Pass level.
Season EXP is earned through daily activity rewards.
When the Season Pass reaches a certain level, you can claim the rewards corresponding to that level.
Adventurers with the Advanced Pass can claim extra rewards at every level of the pass.

400006.Title: Emblem Upgrade Guide
Content: Emblem
Raising your Season Title will unlock the corresponding exclusive Emblem. Emblems not only have visual appearances, but can also be upgraded.
Emblem Upgrade

Each Emblem consists of 1 Emblem Core and 6 power nodes.
The 6 power nodes carry different attributes and effects. Adventurers can upgrade the nodes that best suit their class, talents, modules, and equipment setup to enhance their combat performance.
The Emblem Core contains multiple stat entries. Each upgrade will randomly enhance one of these entries. Adventurers can select up to three stat entries to equip, based on their class, talents, modules, and equipment.

400007.Title: Profile Editing
Content: Profile Editing

1. In the game, you can collect avatars, avatar frames, namecards, titles, and badges, all of which can be viewed and applied in the Edit Profile section.
2. Avatars and avatar frames are displayed in chat channels, team lists, member lists, and other areas. Visit the Guild ID Machine to take a photo and set a custom avatar.
3. Tap on an avatar to view the player's namecard.
4. Titles are displayed above the character's name and on the namecard.
5. Set up your Badge Wall to showcase your badges in your personal space.

400008.Title: Adventurer Honors
Content: Adventurer Honors

1. Earn badges by participating in gameplay activities. You can check how to obtain them in the Badge Gallery.
2. In the Badge Wall Editing interface, you can display the badges you've collected and freely adjust their positions.
3. The Badge Wall is showcased in your Personal Space.

400010.Title: Achievements
Content: Achievement
Achievement progress is never lost. Every effort you make becomes a mark of growth, a witness to your glorious journey through the world.
Achievement Rewards
Completing achievements rewards Rose Orbs (Bound), and many also grant unique titles!

400011.Title: About Phantom Factor Dismantle
Content: Phantom Factor Dismantle

Excess Phantom Factor items can be dismantled. Dismantled items disappear and grant special materials.
Materials obtained from dismantling Phantom Factors can be exchanged for other desired Phantom Factor items. (Note: The total number of Imagine and Will items dismantled in a single action cannot exceed 300.)

400012.Title: About Phantom Factor Selection
Content: About Phantom Factor Selection

Different Phantom Cursors hold different Phantom Factors. View the details on the right panel after selecting the cursor.
Only one Phantom Factor of the same type can be embedded within the same Mind Projection.
Note: Only the Phantom Factors compatible with this Phantom Cursor are shown here.

400013.Title: About Battle Imagine Selection
Content: About Battle Imagine Selection

Battle Imagines of different qualities grant different Bond Points. For the same Battle Imagine, the higher the tier, the higher the Bond Points.
Any activated Battle Imagine can be inserted. Inserted Battle Imagines remain usable and are not affected by this system.
Note: Only activated Battle Imagines are shown here.

400014.Title: Emblem Description
Content: Emblem
Raising your Season Title will unlock the corresponding exclusive Emblem. Emblems not only have visual appearances, but can also be upgraded.
Emblem Upgrade

An Emblem consists of 1 Emblem Core and 6 power nodes.
The 6 power nodes carry different attributes and effects. Adventurers can upgrade the nodes that best suit their class, talents, modules, and gear setup to enhance their combat performance.
The Emblem Core contains multiple stat entries. Each upgrade will randomly enhance one of these entries. Adventurers can select up to 3 stat entries to equip, based on their class, talents, modules, and gear.

400015.Title: About Deep-Slumber Psychoscope
Content: Deep-Slumber Psychoscope Overview

1. Origin and Effects of the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope: The Deep-Slumber Psychoscope is an external mental-tuning device originating from the Bapharian Divines, later modified by artisans. When an adventurer links with the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope, it stabilizes the mind, resisting hallucinations and awakening the adventurer's subconscious power, making them stronger.
2. Deep-Slumber Psychoscope Progression: The Psychoscope system is composed of the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope and the Slumberdream Psychoscope. Each Psychoscope contains multiple Mind Projection areas. Adventurers can select and activate 1 area under each Psychoscope. Each Mind Projection area contains multiple Anchors of the Mind, Phantom Cursors, and Psychogram Imprint nodes (areas under the Slumberdream Psychoscope do not have Psychogram Imprint nodes). By cultivating these nodes, adventurers can enhance their strength.
3. Applicable Environments: The effects of the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope apply in all combat environments, while the effects of the Slumberdream Psychoscope apply only in the Slumberdream Valley.
Mind Projection Overview

1. Different Mind Projection areas contain different Anchors of the Mind, Phantom Cursors, and Psychogram Imprints. Adventurers may select the Mind Projection that best suits their class, talents, modules, and gear setup.
2. Mind Projections that are not activated can still be cultivated, but only activated projections take effect.
3. Locked Mind Projections can only be previewed and cannot be cultivated or activated.
Anchor of the Mind Overview
Anchors of the Mind have fixed effects and can be activated by consuming items.
Phantom Cursor Overview

1. Each Phantom Cursor is essentially a slot for embedding Phantom Factors. However, each cursor has restrictions on what types of factors it can hold. View the details on the right panel after selecting the cursor.
2. The types of Factors a Phantom Cursor can hold may also depend on the adventurer's class. View the details on the right panel after selecting the cursor.
3. Phantom Factors can be removed after being embedded.
4. Phantom Factors: Phantom Factors are special items that carry effects. When embedded in an Phantom Cursor, their effects are transferred to the Mind Projection. Once that projection is activated, the effects apply to the character. Excessive Phantom Factors can be dismantled.
Psychogram Imprint Overview

1. Each Mind Projection area carries one Psychogram Imprint effect, available in multiple levels.
2. Each Psychogram Imprint is essentially a slot that allows insertion of an activated Battle Imagine (Note: Inserted Battle Imagines remain usable and are not affected by this system.)
3. Battle Imagines provide Bond Points to the Psychogram Imprint (Battle Imagines of different qualities grant different Bond Points. For the same Battle Imagine, the higher the tier, the higher the Bond Points.)
4. When a Battle Imagine is inserted into a Psychogram Imprint, Bond Points are added to the Mind Projection. When the total Bond Points across all Psychogram Imprints in that projection reach 2/5/12/20/25/35, the effects of the corresponding Psychogram Imprint level are activated.
5. Note: Areas under the Slumberdream Psychoscope do not have Psychogram Imprint nodes.
Reset Mind Projections

By consuming a special item, adventurers may reset a cultivated Mind Projection. After the reset, all Anchors of the Mind in that projection return to an inactive state, and all items spent to activate them are refunded. All Phantom Factors embedded in Cursors of the Mind are also removed and returned to the Inventory.
About Phantom Factor Dismantle

Excess Phantom Factor items can be dismantled. Dismantled items disappear and grant special materials.
Materials obtained from dismantling Phantom Factors can be exchanged for other desired Phantom Factor items. (Note: The total number of Imagine and Will items dismantled in a single action cannot exceed 300.)
Precautions

1. Only one node in the same tier can be selected.
2. In Slumberdream Valley, neither the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope nor the Slumberdream Psychoscope allows projection switching or resetting. Adventurers must configure the Mind Projection they wish to use before entering the Slumberdream Valley.

400016.Title: Class Loot & Pre-Battle Growth Loadout
Content: Class Loot Rules
Adventurers can set another unlocked class as their loot class. When obtaining growth items for the current class, items for the selected loot class will be received instead.
Pre-Battle Growth Loadout

Players may configure different growth loadouts for different situations, and freely switch between them when needed.
Each growth loadout includes: Class, Talents, Skills, Battle Imagine, Gear, and Modules​. Adventurers can customize each loadout freely.
Loadout Unlocking

Each character is granted a number of free loadout pages. Additional pages may be unlocked using items.
You can assign a custom name to unlocked presets.
Switching & Saving Loadout
Players may switch between loadouts to adapt to different combat scenarios. However, each loadout must be saved before it can be used. And you cannot switch between loadouts during combat.
Special Note on Shared Talent Items

Talent Points Sharing: All Talent Points used in the Talent System are shared across all loadouts.
"Arcane Seals" Sharing When allocating Talent Points, Arcane Seals consumed are stored in an Arcane Seal Container. (Attention: Only Arcane Seals spent when unlocking talents are deposited into the container.) Once stored, all Talent Loadouts share this total value. Example: If you spend "Arcane Seal - Attack" x10 while unlocking talents in Loadout Page 1, all 10 Seals are placed into the "Arcane Seal - Attack" Container. When switching to another loadout and unlocking talents requiring the same type, the system will deduct those 10 Seals in that Container first. Only after the Container is empty will new "Arcane Seal - Attack" Seals be consumed and added to the Container.
Attention: Any Arcane Seals added into the Container cannot be returned to your inventory. They exist solely for Talent System sharing and cannot be withdrawn.

300010.Title: The guild's cargo operations
Content: Guild Cargo

The Guild cargo traders travel around, facilitating the exchange of goods among tribes.
Once the minimum cargo load is reached, they can be manually dispatched. If the minimum load is met, they will automatically depart at 04:00(UTC-2) daily. Once dispatched, the trader will earn a certain amount of Guild Tokens and Luno Ambers.
The Guild cargo trader returns at 05:00(UTC-2) daily and will bring back cargo rewards.
If the cargo load reaches specific thresholds, you'll earn corresponding dispatch rewards.

500109.Title: Weekly Guild Activity
Content: Weekly Guild Activity

Once you join a guild, weekly guild activity will begin, with rewards and quests refreshing at 05:00(UTC-2) every Monday.
Guild members contribute to Guild Merit by participating in events and being active.
Guild sign-ins and Guild cargo can only be completed once per day. Check-ins, shipping, and hunting quests require the Guild Center to be constructed first!
Member Activity Merit refers to the activity value players earn after joining a guild.
About Rewards

When the Weekly Guild Activity reaches certain thresholds, corresponding rewards will be unlocked.
