All guild members are eligible to claim these rewards, provided they have been in the guild for a required period of time. Each reward tier can only be claimed once per member and cannot be claimed multiple times.

500106.Title: Guild Construction
Content: Guild Construction

After joining a Guild, members must sign and contribute together to unlock the Guild Center. Once unlocked, you can enter the Activity Center to begin construction.
You can build and upgrade facilities such as the Guild Center, Boon Fountain, Training Room, Leisure Area, Grocery Store, Photo Panel, and ID Machine.
Leveling up each facility will unlock different features-come build together with your guildmates!

500108.Title: Guild Events
Content: Guild Activities
Various guild events are held regularly, including Guild Hunt and Guild Dance-join in and be part of the action!
Guild Hunt
Guild members must team up to take on Hunting Missions issued by the Pioneer Bureau. Complete the objectives to earn rewards!
Guild Dance
During the event period, go to the second-floor stage area to dance for the Guild Dance. After dancing for a certain amount of time, you will receive corresponding rewards!

5001062.Title: Guild Boon Fountain
Content: Boon Fountain

Once the Boon Fountain is unlocked, Guild Administrators can activate different buff effects using guild funds. Each effect lasts for 24 hours after activation and will expire automatically when the time is up.
Current available buffs include:
For 24 hours, gathering plants in the wild yields 1 extra item.
For 24 hours, obtaining meat from the wild yields 1 extra item.

5001065.Title: Grocery Store
Content: Grocery Store

Upgrading the Guild Center unlocks the Grocery Store, where you can purchase items on the second floor of the Guild Center.
By leveling up the Grocery Store, more items will become available for purchase using Guild Tokens.

50010621.Title: Guild Boon Fountain
Content: Boon Fountain

Once the Boon Fountain is unlocked, Guild Administrators can activate different buff effects using guild funds. Each effect lasts for 24 hours after activation and will expire automatically when the time is up.
Current available buffs include:
For 24 hours, gathering plants in the wild yields 1 extra item.
For 24 hours, obtaining meat from the wild yields 1 extra item.

30045.Title: Rewards
Content: Normal Mode Rewards
Players can take on Normal Mode in either Team or Solo/Dual. In Solo/Dual challenge, Support Companions will be added to assist you in the challenge.
Fate Keystone
Players can obtain rewards by acting as the captain, using a Keystone to start a Fate Mode stage, and successfully completing it.
Rewards

When participating in a Fate Mode stage as a team member, players who successfully complete the stage will roll dice with their Allies for a chance to win a Lucky Reward. Those who do not win the Lucky Reward will still receive a consolation prize.

30046.Title: Trial Path
Content: Introduction
Trial is a stage designed to test your Class Skills. Here, you'll learn the roles and responsibilities of each class in combat, as well as unlock access to more advanced techniques.
Attack
Learn the role and advanced mechanics of Attack classes in combat stages. Your goal is to eliminate enemies as quickly as possible while ensuring your own survival.
Guardian
Learn the role and advanced mechanics of Protection classes in combat stages. Your main duties are to draw enemy attention, protect your Allies, and absorb incoming DMG.
Support

Learn the role and advanced mechanics of Support classes in combat stages. Your main duties include keeping Allies and allies alive, cleansing debuffs, and supporting the party against powerful enemies and harsh environments.
Reward Explanation

Clear Rewards: On first completion, players can receive rewards based on 1-star and 2-star clears. Rewards include: Luno (Bound), Rose Orbs (Bound), and seals required for learning talents.
Goal Rewards: Clearing trials in different roles will grant rewards based on specific achievement conditions. Rewards primarily consist of Talent Points, which are essential for learning talents. Don't forget to claim them!
Monster Difficulty
Monsters in Trial Path have fixed attributes. Their HP and attributes do not change based on power rating or player count.

80001.Title: Trading Center
Content: Item Purchase

1. Players can spend Luno to purchase items.
2. Some products will enter a preview period before release. During this time, players can view upcoming store items and follow those they're interested in.
3. Purchased items will enter a CD period, during which they cannot be listed for sale.
Item Sale

1. Players can list up to 6 items for sale at a time. Item prices can only be adjusted within a specified range. Buying a Monthly Card lets you list 2 extra items for sale. When the Monthly Card expires, you'll need to remove the items in those extra slots before you can view them again.
2. Each listing requires a Luno deposit. The deposit will be returned to the player's store upon a successful transaction or when the listing expires. However, if the player manually removes the item, the deposit will not be refunded.
3. A transaction fee will be charged for each successful sale, based on the final sale amount.
4. Listed items will appear in the market zone after a short delay period.
Item Consignment

1. Players can consign Rose Orb or purchase Rose Orb (Bound) in the Consignment Zone.
2. Consigned Rose Orbs cannot be delisted repeatedly within a short period. This restriction does not apply to listings that have expired.
3. Due to real-time inventory changes, the actual quantity of Rose Orb (Bound) purchased may be less than the amount set by the player.
4. A transaction fee will be charged for successful consignments, based on the sale amount.

800800.Title: Store
Content: Themed Bundle

From time to time, especially during holidays, the shop will launch special value packs at limited-time discounts. These bundles are often sold at just a fraction of their original price-don't miss out!
Style Vault: 90% Off
The store occasionally features limited-time fashion items at 90 percent off. If you enjoy dressing up your character, don't miss out on these deals!
Monthly Card

When purchasing the Monthly Card, you will instantly receive 300 Rose Orbs, an exclusive monthly headpiece, and a Monthly Card. You will also enjoy 30 days of daily rebates and 30 days of Monthly Card privileges. Daily rebates include Rose Orbs (Bound) and Luno (Unbound), while Monthly Card privileges include increased Trading Centers, reduced tax fees, free listing Deposits, and expanded access to Homestead Shared Storage. All details can be viewed in the Monthly Card interface.
Fashion Store

The Fashion Store offers a wide variety of appearance boxes, outfits, and individual pieces. Some items can even be purchased with Rose Orbs (Bound)! If you love customizing your look, this is a place you won't want to miss!
Note: If you've already bought parts of a discounted set, you'll only pay the discounted price for what's left.
Mystery Store
The Mystery Store refreshes daily at 05:00, with a chance to offer discounted items. Don't forget to check in every day-you won't want to miss out on something great!
Colorful Store

The Colorful Store includes the Honor Coin Store, Reputation Store, and Friendship point Store, each using a different type of currency for purchases. When you have extra currencies on hand, don't forget to check in-you might find something you need!
Meow Store
Meowlux Merchant Guild Picks include the Premium Store and the Vault Store, each using a different type of token for purchases. For more details, head over to the Guild and check it out!
Other Notes
Please enjoy in moderation and make purchases rationally.

800801.Title: Meowlux Special Guide
Content: Meowlux Special Guide

Meowlux Special is a brand-new draw event launched by the Meowlux Merchant Guild. Its initiator is none other than the heiress whose ideas run faster than common sense—Meowclu.
She has always believed that the world in dreams does not disappear upon waking, but simply continues from a different angle. The odd gadgets, sparkling accessories, and cute items with unknown uses she encounters in her dreams deserve a chance to meet everyone in reality. Thus, Meowclu began attempting to recreate the things she remembered from her dreams.
Seeing the heiress's enthusiasm, the Guild's staff organized these "little gifts from another world" into a draw event named "Meowlux Special"!
Meowclu often says that dreams cannot be replicated, but wishes can. The Meowlux Special is her way of wrapping wishes as gifts.
Draw Rules

1. Draw Cost: Typically, each draw consumes a certain number of Gold Meow Points. However, there may be free attempts initially. Additionally, during the draw process, you may trigger a "Next Draw Free" Lucky Event. Gold Meow Points can be obtained for free via the "Starry Carnival" at the top right of the prize pool, or exchanged through the quick‑purchase window that appears during draws.
2. Adventurers can choose to draw 1 time or 5 times per attempt.
3. Each draw yields one prize. As long as you have enough draws, you are guaranteed to empty the prize pool. Note that as draws progress, the Draw Chance of each prize changes dynamically. When the remaining available draws for a prize reach 0, its weight is removed from the prize pool, causing the Draw Chance of the remaining prizes to change.
4. By Lady Meowclu's special favor, draws may trigger Prize Pool Events, including Basic Events and Lucky Events.
Basic Events Info

Basic Events include:
1. Every draw guarantees a certain amount of Gold Meow Points rebates. The rebate amount depends on the rarity of the prize drawn.
2. Guaranteed High-Tier Prize: When adventurers reach a specified number of draws, they are guaranteed to receive a high-tier prize.
Lucky Events Info

1. Lucky Events include: Next Draw Free, Next Draw Returns N-times Gold Meow Points, Next Draw Increased Draw Chance for Specified Rarity, Next Draw Excludes Specified Rarity, etc.
2. Lucky Events appear randomly with each draw. It is possible to trigger no Lucky Event, but the Gold Meow Point rebate from Basic Events is always guaranteed.
Note: If a triggered Lucky Event is related to a specific prize type that has already been emptied, the event will be considered invalid. For example, if "Probability of Prize A increased by 10%" is triggered but Prize A is sold out, the event is invalid.
Special Info

1. Prize pools and their corresponding exchange store are usually available for a limited time. Adventurers should pay attention to the end time. If you miss them, you can visit Meownia at the Cat's Eye Emporium on Starland to recycle expired Gold Meow Points and exchange items.
2. If you choose multiple draws, Gold Meow Points are deducted first (if the next draw is free, the free count is used directly and Gold Meow Points are not deducted). If a "Next Draw Free" Lucky Event is triggered in the middle of a multiple draw, the free draw count generated will be converted into Gold Meow Points and refunded after the draws are completed.
3. The displayed draw probabilities for all prizes are truncated to four decimal places (not rounded). It is normal if the sum of all probabilities is slightly less than 100%.

7050.Title: Mount Imagine
Content: Mount Imagine

Mount Imagines are loyal companions to adventurers.
Not only do they offer faster Speed, but each Mount Imagine also comes with its own unique abilities, such as Rainbow Leap or Anti-Gravity.
Those abilities are perfect for assisting you on your adventures. Some even have the power to launch Allies into the air, just waiting to be discovered!
However, there are no Mount Imagines capable of swimming. Please stay tuned for future updates!

400201.Title: Ability Score Explanation
Content: Ability Score

The Ability Score is a reference value used to assess your strength, reflecting your current development progress.

The main ways to increase your Ability Score include: Adventurer Level, Talent Development, Skill Development, Gear Development, Module Development, and the Deep-Slumber Psychoscope feature. Developing these systems effectively raises your Ability Score.
Precautions

The ability score is only a reference value. In actual game experience, the player's true strength is also affected by combat scenarios, player status, team cooperation, and other factors. Players should refer to it rationally!

400202.Title: Ability Evaluation Explanation
Content: Evaluate

Ability evaluation is a simple reference panel based on the combat scenario and one's own strength. Players can make simple comparisons with their ability scores and key attributes to evaluate their combat strength.
Precautions

Ability evaluation is only a reference. In actual game experience, the player's true strength is also affected by combat scenarios, personal status, team cooperation, and other factors. Players should make their own judgments based on their situation and team conditions!

400009.Title: Meowlux Merchant Guild Overview
Content: Meow Store
The Meowlux Merchant Guild offers a selection of premium fashion items, which are updated from time to time. Fashion lovers, don't miss out!
Premium Store
Purchases in the Premium Store require Meowlux Premium Cards, which can be obtained through the Wish feature.
Member Store
Purchases in the Member Store require Meow Coins. Increase your Meowlux membership level to claim monthly Boxes, which contain Meow Coins.
Vault Store
Purchases in the Vault Store require Meowlux Vault Cards, which can be earned via Wish and may also appear in the Mystery Store.

14500.Title: Wall Run
Content: Wall Run is an ability that allows you to run along walls and pause briefly while on them.
Stamina is consumed while running on walls. If your stamina runs out, you will no longer be able to remain on the wall.

500110.Title: Guild Crowdfunding
Content: Guild Crowdfunding

After creating a Guild, the Guild Center must be unlocked through a Signature Crowdfunding event. This event becomes available at 05:00(UTC-2) on the second day after the server opens.
Once the event starts, tap the [Go to Crowdfunding] button to enter the Signature Crowdfunding interface. You'll need 10 members to sign. Once the crowdfunding is successful, construction of the Guild Center will begin!
Guild Center Construction

Once the Guild Signature Crowdfunding is completed, a 10-minute construction period for the Guild Center will begin. After construction is finished, you can enter the Guild Center by tapping the [Guild Center] button in this area!

500200.Title: Guild Dance
Content: Participation Guide

1. During the event period, guild members can go to the second-floor stage area to dance. After dancing for a certain amount of time, players will receive personal rewards.
2. The more players join the stage to dance, the more the stage lighting will change dynamically.
3. Continuous dancing will fill the Guild Dance progress bar. Reaching certain milestones will grant bonus effects in the Guild Hunt gameplay.
4. While dancing, a character named Ee-chan will randomly appear in different locations within the guild. Talk to her to receive extra rewards, which are linked to the results of your last Hunt session.

500300.Title: Stimen Vaults Description
Content: Challenge Guide

1. The ruins consist of 60 floors. The first 20 will be open at the beginning of the season, with an additional 10 floors opening every 2 weeks until all 60 are available. Each floor grants a reward upon first successful completion.
2. Players can take on the challenge solo or in a team. Teaming up makes the challenge easier.
3. Players can only start the challenge from the first floor of each stage. Upon the successful completion of the last floor of a stage, the starting point will move up to the first floor of the next stage.
4. In team challenges, if team members have different starting floors, the team can only start from the lowest starting floor among them.
Reset

Each Ruins cycle lasts for two weeks. Every Monday from 02:00 to 05:00 (UTC-2) is the weekly reset period, during which entry is unavailable. After reset, your current progress, floor rewards, and cycle objectives will be reset. If you miss any rewards during the current cycle, you can recover them in the next cycle via the Reclaim Hub.
Boss Reset
Elites and bosses within the Ruins reset every two weeks. They may reappear in the same form or change upon reset.
Monster Attributes

In the Ruins, attributes of monsters on each floor scale only with the number of players entering and are not affected by player Power Score or server age. A new mechanic, Illusion-Breaking Energy check, will be introduced in the new season—players who do not meet the required Illusion-Breaking Energy will receive a debuff.
Skip Floors

When the reset happens every 2 weeks, players can skip a certain number of floors based on how many floors they have unlocked and their highest cleared floor this season. Players can claim skip-floor rewards for the corresponding floors directly. You cannot start challenges in the new cycle until these have been claimed.

500111.Title: Item Recycling Description
Content: Item Recycling

Items obtained from recycling are bound.
Items cannot be returned after recycling, please operate with caution.

801200.Title: Monthly Card Rules
Content: Monthly Card Cycle
The Monthly Card remains valid for 30 days.
Monthly Card Benefits
Upon purchasing the Monthly Card, you immediately receive purchase rewards, a collection card, 30 days of daily returns, and 30 days of Monthly Card privileges.
Reward Explanation

Purchase Reward: Activating the Monthly Card instantly grants you the purchase rewards and the current month's collection card.
Monthly Card Privilege: Once the Monthly Card is activated, its privileges and daily returns become immediately available.
Refresh Instructions

You can purchase the Monthly Card for up to 3 months, with refreshes occurring at the start of each calendar month.
Upon activating the Monthly Card, the duration of privileges and returns immediately accumulates.
Monthly exclusive content is delivered on the first day of each month.
Daily Rebate Special Notes

1. When you activate the first Monthly Card you purchase, you will immediately receive the current day's reward. After that, the system refreshes at 05:00 (UTC-2) server time daily. Adventurers who log in after 05:00 (UTC-2) will receive the new day's monthly card reward.
2. If your online sessions goes through 05:00, you will receive the monthly card reward next time you switch scenes or login.
3. If you do not log in that day, the monthly card rewards will accumulate and be fully granted upon the next login.
Note: There may be a delay between when the Monthly Card reward is issued and when the popup appears. That is: When you see the popup, the reward has already been granted.

During your adventures, you'll come across countless breathtaking sights. Some photography enthusiasts have turned famous landmarks into collectible postcards that have been widely circulated.

2101.Title: Life Skill
Content: Life Skill

There are two Life Skills: Gathering and Crafting. Crafting lets you use materials gathered through Gathering to create a wide variety of useful items. With Life Skills, adventurers can craft items for sale at the Trading Center to earn Luno, or produce their own gear, furniture, and more.

Gathering and Crafting actions come in two types: Focused and Regular. Performing a Focused Action consumes Focus, while a Regular Action costs nothing.

Focused Actions are usually unlocked by learning a Mastery. Every 300 Focus consumed earns you 1 Mastery point. Mastery points are shared across all Life Skills. If you're unhappy with your chosen Mastery, you can reset them individually per Class.

Regular Actions are typically unlocked by increasing your Life Skill Level. Weekly Supply Quests are a major source of Life Skills EXP. Each Focused or Crafting Action grants a large amount of EXP. Even Regular Actions will grant a certain amount of EXP.

There are no limits on how many Life Skills you can unlock. Adventurers are free to unlock them all. Unlocking any Life Skill for the first time grants 3 Mastery points and 2 Mastery Reset Tickets.

Need a certain Life Skill material in a hurry? Try visiting the Exchange Store of each Class's instructor.

Some Alchemy recipes need to be unlocked through Recipe Experimentation.

Experiments can only use specific Alchemy materials, and the recipe pool varies for different Alchemy materials.

After placing Alchemy materials into the Alchemy furnace, you can check the question mark area on the interface to view the recipe pool and its unlocking status for that material.

Focused: You can create fashion dyes, which may be traded at the Trading Center or utilized for personal purposes. The materials required for crafting these items are primarily sourced from Botany and Alchemy.

Regular: You can create non-tradable items such as medium armor, light armor, charm, homestead construction materials, and cloth/leather furniture. The materials required for crafting are primarily sourced from Botany gathering and hunting, or further processed via the Weaving, Culinary, and Smelting professions.

2116.Title: Life Skill - Buy Now
Content: Buy Now

When all required materials for crafting an item can be purchased from the Life Skill Store or Homestead Store, you may use Buy Now. The system automatically calculates the required quantity, deducts materials already in your inventory, and purchases the remaining materials while calculating the total cost. You may choose to pay with Luno (Bound) or Homestead Coins.

2117.Title: Life Skill - Gathering Commission
Content: Introduction

For non-focused gathering targets, you can spend a small amount of Homestead Coins to issue a Gathering Commission, allowing NPCs to collect resources on your behalf. Select the target and quantity, submit the commission, and collect the rewards from the NPC once the time is up.

2118.Title: Homestead Smelting Bench
Content: Homestead Smelting Bench Guide

Use the Smelting Bench's production queue to craft non-Focus materials for furniture making at the cost of only a small amount of Homestead Coins. You can leave the Homestead during production and return later to collect the finished materials.

2119.Title: Homestead Alchemy Bench
Content: Homestead Alchemy Bench Guide

Use the Alchemy Bench's production queue to craft non-Focus materials for furniture making at the cost of only a small amount of Homestead Coins. You can leave the Homestead during production and return later to collect the finished materials.

2120.Title: Homestead Gemcrafting Bench
Content: Homestead Gemcrafting Bench Guide

Use the Gemcrafting Bench's production queue to craft non-Focus materials for furniture making at the cost of only a small amount of Homestead Coins. You can leave the Homestead during production and return later to collect the finished materials.

2121.Title: Homestead Artisanry Bench
Content: Homestead Artisanry Bench Guide

Use the Artisanry Bench's production queue to craft non-Focus materials for furniture making at the cost of only a small amount of Homestead Coins. You can leave the Homestead during production and return later to collect the finished materials.

2122.Title: Homestead Weaving Bench
Content: Homestead Weaving Bench Guide

Use the Weaving Bench's production queue to craft non-Focus materials for furniture making at the cost of only a small amount of Homestead Coins. You can leave the Homestead during production and return later to collect the finished materials.

100000.Title: Backpack Instructions
Content: Backpack Instructions

The Backpack will automatically store all consumables obtained. Adventurers can choose whether to enable automatic storage, and can also drag consumables from the left side into the portable bag to edit its contents. After editing, consumables can be used easily from the main interface.

5050.Title: Life Skills - Culinary Overview
Content: Life Skill

There are two Life Skills: Gathering and Crafting. Crafting lets you use materials gathered through Gathering to create a wide variety of useful items. With Life Skills, adventurers can craft items for sale at the Trading Center to earn Luno, or produce their own gear, furniture, and more.

Gathering and Crafting actions come in two types: Focused and Regular. Performing a Focused Action consumes Focus, while a Regular Action costs nothing.

Focused Actions are usually unlocked by learning a Mastery. Every 300 Focus consumed earns you 1 Mastery point. Mastery points are shared across all Life Skills. If you're unhappy with your chosen Mastery, you can reset them individually per Class.

Regular Actions are typically unlocked by increasing your Life Skill Level. Weekly Supply Quests are a major source of Life Skills EXP. Each Focused or Crafting Action grants a large amount of EXP. Even Regular Actions will grant a certain amount of EXP.

There are no limits on how many Life Skills you can unlock. Adventurers are free to unlock them all. Unlocking any Life Skill for the first time grants 3 Mastery points and 2 Mastery Reset Tickets.

Need a certain Life Skill material in a hurry? Try visiting the Exchange Store of each Class's instructor.
Culinary Recipe

Focused: You can create cuisines that enhance stats and Cheer-Up Treats, which may be traded at the Trading Center or utilized for personal purposes. The materials required for crafting these items are primarily sourced from Botany and Fishing.

Regular: You can create non-tradable items such as bait and Fertilizer. The materials required for crafting these items are primarily gathered through Botany and fishing, or further processed via the Culinary profession.

2401.Title: Combo Action Editor
Content: Combo Action Editor

The Combo Action Editor features two tracks: Actions and Emotes. You can freely add unlocked actions and emotes to the tracks and adjust their start and end times to create a unique combo action.

Once saved, combo actions can be used in the open world conveniently. Several Combo Action Slots are available by default; additional slots must be unlocked using specific items.

Some actions do not currently support customization.

2402.Title: Actions Archive
Content: Actions Archive

Actions and Emotes are a unique way to express yourself when interacting with others. Through the Combo Action Editor, you can freely combine actions and emotes to showcase your exclusive personality! The

Actions Archive lets you view your current collection progress for all actions and emotes, as well as how to unlock them.

2500.Title: Pioneer Award
Content: Introduction

Each reward type offers 3 goals per week. Complete any one of them to choose a reward the following Monday. For reward rows with multiple stars, you can upgrade the entire row's reward quality by completing specific goals.
Rewards are randomly selected from gear, modules, and materials. Modules only appear after the feature is unlocked. Gear, Reforge Stone, and other improve in item level and quality as the server progresses.
Players may choose one reward per type each week.

2501.Title: Pioneer Award - Chaotic Realm
Content: Introduction
Complete any Chaotic Realm dungeon.

23001.Title: Renewal Store Introduction
Content: Renewal Store

After participating in the Renewal Festival event series, you can use Renewal Seals and Renewal Light Vault Cards to exchange for exclusive festival-themed rewards in the Renewal Store!

※After the Renewal Light wish event ends, Renewal Light Vault Card exchanges will be available in the Renewal Store. Unused Renewal Light Wish Coins can be converted into Renewal Light Vault Cards at a 1:5 ratio.
※The Renewal Store entrance on the Renewal Festival event page will remain open until 5:00 (UTC-2) on Mar. 12, 2026. Please redeem limited-time items before it closes.
※ Kalista at the Meowlux Member Store will continue to provide access to the Renewal Store, where you can exchange non–limited-time items.

2502.Title: Pioneer Award - Wild World
Content: Introduction
Defeat elite or boss monsters in the open world.

2503.Title: Pioneer Award - Life Skill Goals
Content: Introduction
To learn a Life Skill, simply consume the corresponding Focus to complete the objective.

2551.Title: Master Gameplay Overview
Content: Introduction

Each cleared stage unlocks a higher difficulty. New difficulty levels open as the server time progresses. The higher the difficulty, the stronger the entries and monsters. Clearing higher difficulties faster increases your maximum score per dungeon. Raising your total Master Mode score earns exclusive seasonal glory rewards.

30041.Title: Membership Level Explanation
Content: Membership Level
Collect new outfits, Mount Imagines, and weapon skins and participate in appearance-related features to gain Membership Points and increase your membership level.
About Points

Quest Points: Earned by joining outfit changes, photo sessions, beauty customization, dyeing, etc. Different tasks offer varying points and limits. Quest Points expire monthly, refreshing at 05:00 (UTC-2) on the 1st. Point cap increases at 05:00 (UTC-2) on the 10th and 20th.
Season Points: Earned when you obtain a new outfit, Mount Imagine, or weapon skin for the first time. Valid for 365 days.
Collection Points: Earned through Collection Rewards and are permanently valid.
Privileges Overview

Curated Stores: Join the Meowlux Merchant Guild to access curated items in the Premium Store, Member Store, and Vault Store.
Monthly Gifts: Meowlux Merchant Guild prepares monthly gifts for members according to their membership level. Refreshes monthly at 05:00(UTC-2) on the 1st to be claimed again according to levels.

2300.Title: Events Store
Content: Event Store
All Event Stores are time-limited and will close when the event ends.

3021.Title: Fireworks Festival Surprise Egg
Content: Festival Surprise Egg
During the celebration, a mysterious guest may appear to watch the fireworks show. Interact with them to make you glow with golden or silver light, or even grow bigger or smaller!

3022.Title: Fireworks Festival Chest
Content: Festival Treasure Chest

Ee-chan Chests will appear during the celebration! Open them to receive rewards and launch an Ee-chan Firework! Each Ee-chan Firework Chest can be opened once throughout the event. There are a total of 45 Ee-chan Firework Chests on the beach. Go find them!

3023.Title: Illusion Event
Content: Recover Caravan Cargo

Within the Illusion, even caravans exist! Every 10 minutes, various cargo will scatter across the realm. Help the caravans recover their goods to increase the Drifting Cocoon's Condensation Energy and earn Dim Dreamstones.
Collect Illusion Inscriptions
Inscription Gift Cooldown
Break the Barrier of Floating Dreams

There are 7 Barriers of Floating Dreams within the Illusion. Adventurers must work together to shatter them. Each barrier demands the combined efforts of 5, 10, or 15 players. Successfully shattering a barrier grants a large amount of the Drifting Cocoon's Condensation Energy. Treasure chests inside will remain for 3 minutes, during which all players can open them to obtain Dim Dreamstones. Each barrier resets 1 hour after being shattered.

3024.Title: Rules
Content: Land of Crimson Illusion
A road that should have led to the Bahamar Highlands instead opens into a fantastical, unreal space—an illusory realm with its own strange cycle of life and transformation.
The Drifting Cocoon

The Drifting Cocoon alternates between the Condensation and Diffusion Phases.

During the Condensation Phase, it generates Condensation Energy every second. By defeating Illusion Monsters or solving Illusion Puzzles, adventurers will grant it additional energy, accelerating its transition into the Diffusion Phase.

During the Diffusion Phase, the Cocoon's energy gradually decays. Illusion Elites and Bosses will appear nearby. Defeating them grants large amounts of Dim Dreamstones and may drop special Entries that enhance your power in the Illusion.

Every 5/10/15 minutes during the Diffusion Phase, special events may occur, such as scattered treasure chests, powerful Illusion Lords, or the emergence of Illusion Beast's Power.
Dim Dreamstone

A special currency generated within the Illusion. Intangible and invisible in the Inventory. You can check your current total Dim Dreamstones via the [Status Bar].

Dim Dreamstones can be used to exchange for Entries at Illusion Ee-chan in the Safe Zone, or trade for rewards at Illusion Nappo!
900 Dim Dreamstones for 90 Seeds of Floating Dreams
200 Dim Dreamstones for 10 Seeds of Floating Dreams
Starting from Week 2, exchanges also grant Shining Seeds of Floating Dreams.

Beware! If you hold over 200 Dreamstones, half will be dropped upon death! Entries and Dim Dreamstones will be cleared upon switching scenes, changing lines, or logging out!
Illusion Level

[The number] shown in front of an Illusion Monster's name indicates its Illusion Level. Exchange corresponding [+Number] Entries at Illusion Ee-chan in the Safe Zone to increase your Illusion Level.

High-level Illusion Monsters will deal massive DMG to adventurers of lower Illusion Level!
Illusion Beast's Power

During the Diffusion Phase, at random event times (every 5/10/15 minutes), the Illusion Beast's Power may appear at random locations. Adventurers can consume Dim Dreamstones to transform into their corresponding class Imagines. This allows them to attack other Adventurers. However, they will drop all their Dim Dreamstones upon death!

Illusion Stormblade
Core Skill 1: Upon sheathing the sword, ignores the first incoming DMG and instantly counters with a Flash strike. Each successful parry grants 1 stack of Ready to Strike.
Core Skill 2: After Dracoflash, triggers an explosive AoE blast. Each Ready to Strike stack increases the explosion DMG by 100%.

Illusion Heavy Guardian
Core Skill 1: Pulls all surrounding players toward the caster, slowing them before charging up for a Rage Burst.
Core Skill 2: Charges in place, then deals massive AoE damage. Each time the Caster takes a hit, this skill's damage increases by 10%.

Illusion Frost Mage
Core Skill 1: Casts a ​Frostbeam​.
Core Skill 2: Summons two Tidal Vortexes which move automatically.
Core Skill 3: Conjures an icy storm around the Caster and initiates chanting. After a while, summons two Water Vortexes that automatically cast Ice Meteors.

3030.Title: Newbie Special Operation
Content: Newbie Special Operation

Newbie Criteria: On Server Day 5, the system will conduct a newbie check, marking all players ≤Lv.29 with a Newbie tag. The Newbie tag will be automatically removed from any player after 28 days of real time.
Newbie Perks: When players with the Newbie tag participate in Unstable Space, Chaotic Realm, Guild Hunt, Stimen Vaults, Chaotic Realm Master, and 20-player raid, Newbie players gain extra Friendship Points. Weekly caps apply.
Even after the Newbie tag expires, any unclaimed rewards in the Newbie Special Operation can still be claimed!
Newbie Privileges

Your stats receive dynamic bonuses:
Activity gain increases by 20%;
Honor Coin gain increases by 20%;
Newbie adventurers gain an additional 50 Friendship Points per run in designated activity (weekly cap applies). Active adventurers assisting newbies also gain 50 Friendship Points per run (weekly cap applies).
With a newbie in the team, treasure monsters are guaranteed to spawn! This doesn't apply to Solo/Duo mode.
Journey Essentials
Complete quests in your current Essential to unlock the next. Finish all Essentials to claim a [1 out of 8 Fashion Pack]!
Daily Exclusive Schedule

Choose your interests to refresh matching quests. You can refresh up to 3 times per day; completed quests will not be refreshed. Quests refresh every day at 05:00 (UTC-2) based on the interests you've currently selected.

3031.Title: Returnee Special Operation
Content: Returnee Special Operation

Returnee Criteria: Players ≥Lv.30 who have been offline for more than 28 days will receive the Returnee Tag upon logging in again. The tag lasts 28 days. Each season, a player can receive the Returnee tag only once.
Returnee Perks: Returnee players can complete Returnee Special Operation tasks to earn generous rewards.
Once your Returnee tag expires, the Returnee Special Operation ends immediately, and the rewards will be delivered to your mailbox. Don't forget to complete tasks and claim your rewards in time.
Returnee Privileges

Your stats receive dynamic bonuses:
Activity gain increases by 20%
Honor Coin gain increases by 20%
Friendship Points Boost: Players with the returnee tag will receive extra Friendship Points when participating in Unstable Space, Chaotic Realm, Guild Hunting, Stimen Vaults, Chaotic Realm - Master, and 20-player raids. A weekly cap applies to these extra Friendship Points.
With a returnee in the team, treasure monsters are guaranteed to spawn! This doesn't apply to Solo/Duo mode.
Daily Gifts
Log in daily as a returnee to claim rewards. On Day 14, you can receive a [1 out of 8 Fashion Pack]!
Daily Exclusive Schedule

Choose your interests to refresh matching quests. You can refresh up to 3 times per day; completed quests will not be refreshed. Quests refresh every day at 05:00 (UTC-2) based on the interests you've currently selected.

3040.Title: Hide-and-Seek Rules
Content: Introduction

5-20 players can join, either via matchmaking or in a party. Adventurers are divided into two factions: Shadow Hunters and Stalkers. Hunters try to capture Stalkers, while Stalkers transform and hide. If all Stalkers are caught within the time limit, the Hunters win; otherwise, the Stalkers claim victory.

3041.Title: Hide-and-Seek Archive Guide
Content: Introduction

5–20 players can join, either via matchmaking or in a party. Adventurers are divided into two factions: Shadow Hunters and Stalkers. Hunters try to capture Stalkers, while Stalkers transform and hide. If all Stalkers are caught within the time limit, the Hunters win; otherwise, the Stalkers claim victory.

23002.Title: "Astral Store" Event Store
Content: Astral Store

Participate in Starry Carnival to earn <color=#eed335>Meowlux Special Universal Tickets</color> and <color=#9cb9ef>Astral Gold Meow Tickets</color>! Exchange them at the Astral Store for limited rewards!

2410.Title: Musician
Content: Musician Proficiency Level

Adventurers can obtain Proficiency for different musicians by completing Daily Practice and Weekly Practice quests to increase their Proficiency, unlocking more instrument functions, sheet music, and level rewards.

Practice quests refresh daily and weekly, and their difficulty is matched to the musician's current Proficiency Level to help Adventurers unfamiliar with real instruments progress step by step.

Daily quest rewards can be claimed up to 1 time per day, and weekly quest rewards can be claimed up to 5 times per week. Adventurers may choose to focus on increasing the Proficiency Level of a single instrument or improve multiple instruments simultaneously.
More Instrument Features

Each instrument is equipped with professional performance features.

For example, the keyboard provides a full range of octaves and a sustain pedal. The electric guitar supports playing techniques such as harmonics and palm muting, and allows you to freely switch between effects like clean, overdrive, and distortion. The electric bass also features various playing techniques and effect switching. These performance features gradually unlock as your musician proficiency level increases.

You also have a built-in smart metronome, allowing you to adjust the beat and tempo based on specific songs and ensemble requirements. The metronome supports both Solo Practice Mode and Group Ensemble Mode. In solo mode, you can use it to hone your skills, while in ensemble mode, you can synchronize the beat for all members, enabling seamless collaboration.
Graded Tutorials

There are <style="GuideBookLab1">graded tutorials</style> of varying difficulties to help Adventurers learn instrument performances and gradually familiarize themselves with notes and keys. Tutorials gradually unlock as your musician proficiency level increases.
Ensemble Feature

The online ensemble feature allows Adventurers to use the Party system to initiate an ensemble in Free Performance mode. During an ensemble, all members can synchronize beats for seamless collaboration.

Parties are not restricted by instrument types, so Adventurers can form ensembles based on the requirements of each song.

3032.Title: Instructor Overview
Content: Instructor Certification Overview

Complete designated tasks in the Instructor Certification to become a Star Instructor. After becoming a certified Star Instructor, pass the seasonal evaluation to be promoted to Sky Instructor and receive the specified rewards.
Instructor Quests

Instructor Weekly Quests: Quests can be completed repeatedly to earn Instructor Points. Reach the required weekly Instructor Points to claim rewards. Rewards reset every Monday at 5:00 AM.
Challenge Quests: Complete the corresponding challenge quests to obtain rewards!
